programme.title: The Key of David
programme.desc: Religious documentary, analysing current events using Bible prophecy

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: Dr Sloan risks his life to investigate a spate of mysterious deaths at a high school, all of which seem to revolve around one particularly 'helpful' student. Dick Van Dyke stars

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: Dr Sloan investigates a fatal car accident, and is baffled when he discovers the dead racing driver had survived a similar mishap only days earlier. Medical detective drama, starring Dick Van Dyke

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: The death of a four-year-old girl forces Dr Sloan to choose between hospital protocol and honour, casting a shadow over his entire career. Drama, starring Dick Van Dyke

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: Sylvester seriously injures himself after triggering an explosive device during an investigation into a gang of thieves. The team must work with Interpol and remain focused on finding the culprits while their friend is in a critical condition. Meanwhile, Drew decides he wants to get close to Paige again. Drama, inspired by a true story, starring Ari Stidham and Brendan Hines

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: The team races against the clock to save a boy who is trapped inside a sinkhole. Walter manages to get Megan into a study researching a new MS drug, but she is not keen on being part of a trial. Meanwhile, Paige tries to bring the festive spirit to the team when Drew is forced to miss Christmas with Ralph for a baseball try-out in Portland. Drama, starring Elyes Gabel, Eddie Kaye Thomas and Camille Guaty

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: While on a joint Coast Guard and NCIS training exercise at sea with Agent Borin, Gibbs spots a dead man overboard, who is found to be a missing member of the crew of a nearby vessel. However, when the agent boards the ship for questioning, he realises it has been commandeered by pirates. Naval drama, starring Mark Harmon, Michael Weatherly and Pauley Perrette

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team investigates the murder of a Navy commander's wife, determining whether it is linked to her role as a therapist, or an act of terrorism since her husband's name has appeared on a Jihadist's target list. Elsewhere, the group discusses Halloween costumes, as well as Tony's recent dating habits. Starring Mark Harmon, Michael Weatherly and Pauley Perrette

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team uncovers a fraudulent charity that preys on those searching for missing military personnel while investigating the murder of a retired Master Sergeant. Meanwhile, Bishop agonises over the results of her probation evaluation, and Tony contemplates adopting a stray cat that followed him home. Starring Mark Harmon, Michael Weatherly and Pauley Perrette

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team investigates the cause of a hit-and-run incident in which a Petty Officer has been killed. Meanwhile, Gibbs works to clear a Navy Hospital Corpsman who has been indicted for illegally providing medical aid to victims of the crash, despite the injured insisting her techniques helped save their lives. Starring Mark Harmon, Michael Weatherly and Pauley Perrette

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: An enigmatic killer shoots dead a businessman spending Thanksgiving at a hotel in the city, then takes his glasses, jewellery and ID to Washington Dulles airport, which has been brought to a stand-still by a massive snowstorm. As the murderer's actions take effect, a terror alert is issued, leaving all northern US airports in a state of panic, and leaving it up to DiNozzo, Bishop and her husband - all of whom are trapped at Dulles by the weather - to bring the armed villain to justice. Crime drama, starring Michael Weatherley

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: Sylvester seriously injures himself after triggering an explosive device during an investigation into a gang of thieves. The team must work with Interpol and remain focused on finding the culprits while their friend is in a critical condition. Meanwhile, Drew decides he wants to get close to Paige again. Drama, inspired by a true story, starring Ari Stidham and Brendan Hines

Meanwhile, Paige tries to bring the festive spirit to the team when Drew is forced to miss Christmas with Ralph for a baseball try-out in Portland. Drama, starring Elyes Gabel, Eddie Kaye Thomas and Camille Guaty

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: While on medical leave, Ducky reopens a 12-year-old case by talking officials at a cemetery into releasing the body of a navy commander whose post-mortem he carried out 12 years previously. Meanwhile, the team is called in to investigate the death of a sergeant from carbon monoxide poisoning - and finds a copy of Ducky's exhumation request on the man's laptop. Crime drama, starring David McCallum

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team is called in to investigate when three dehydrated survivors from a helicopter crash are discovered. The pilot is initially missing, but his body later washes ashore with a gunshot wound to the head. Things get complicated when one of the crew members tells the agents the chopper was hit by another aircraft, while another states that the pilot tried to kill them all by crashing into the sea on purpose. American drama, starring Mark Harmon and David McCallum

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The driver of a Ferrari is gunned down by a motorcyclist and the victim is identified as petty officer Colin Boxer, who was holding down a second job as a valet parker and had borrowed the sports car. The owner of the vehicle turns out to be Vijay Chaya, the billionaire head of a large corporation, and the team suspects a case of mistaken identity. During the course of the investigation, Gibbs chances on a medal awarded to Leroy Jethro Moore, the man he was named after and an estranged friend of his father. Guest starring Billy Dee Williams (The Empire Strikes Back) and Ralph Waite (The Waltons)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Part one of two. First lieutenant Michael Torres gets involved in a fight before falling to the ground, and is found beaten to death the next morning. The only witness is his friend and colleague Captain Joe Westcott, who suffers from post-traumatic stress disorder and has trouble distinguishing reality from his past military experiences in the Middle East

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Part two of two. Tony reviews satellite footage of rebel forces in the Middle East and spots Kersey among a group of insurgents, concluding that Joe Westcott's fear of him was well-founded. Back at HQ, Abby creates a 3D computer model of the attack that occurred in Afghanistan and Joe dons a virtual-reality headset to relive the traumatic events. Will it jog his memory?

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: Dr Sloan gets mixed up in a conman's attempt to extort $300,000 from the hospital - but has trouble proving the villain's guilt. Medical detective drama, starring Dick Van Dyke

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: Dr Sloan puts on his thinking cap to investigate a murder at a benefactors' ball being chaired by Jesse. Drama, starring Dick Van Dyke

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: Dr Sloan's detective work results in the execution of a suspected serial bomber, but as the blasts continue he fears he's made a fatal mistake. Feature-length special of the crime drama, starring Dick and Barry Van Dyke, with Victoria Rowell

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: The team members assist Ralph when he unlocks a secret level in a dark website video game, and it unintentionally reveals the location of a CIA safe house. Meanwhile, Paige confronts her feelings for Walter. Drama, inspired by a true story, starring Elyes Gabel, Eddie Kaye Thomas and Jadyn Wong

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Inquiries into the death of a marine land the team members squarely in the middle of an inner-city gang war - and a message found at the scene of the crime seemingly implicates Gibbs. Forced to confront a secret from his past, he calls his mentor Mike Franks for help. Drama, guest starring Muse Watson and Kari Coleman, with Mark Harmon

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: One of Tony's old murder investigations is reopened and, in an attempt to find the perpetrator, he decides to switch roles with Gibbs, leading the team in an inquiry that links the case to an embezzlement scheme. American drama, starring Mark Harmon, David McCallum, Sasha Alexander, Michael Weatherly and Pauley Perrette

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team heads into the Arizona desert to locate an artist who might be able to help solve the murder of fellow agent Jack Patterson - and discovers one of her paintings holds the key to the mystery. Meanwhile, DiNozzo learns he is in line for an inheritance

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Vance insists on taking charge of an investigation into the fatal shooting of a boxer - but antagonises Gibbs in the process, and finds his own past coming under scrutiny as the case unfolds. Drama, starring Mark Harmon and Rocky Carroll

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The family of a child who finds a gun suspected of being a murder weapon at a naval base comes under suspicion. The team sets out to discover who last used the firearm, with likely candidates including a petty criminal, a drug user, a Navy commander and a boy. Crime drama, starring Pauley Perrette and Mark Harmon

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Three men stage a raid on a drug deal, taking a suitcase full of cash and killing the dealer and an undercover LAPD officer. While studying footage taken from CCTV equipment in the house, Sam recognises the distinctive hand signals of one of the suspects as being those of Navy Seal Kurt Holgate, who he served with in Bosnia. Eric studies the details of his unit and finds that a man called Jackson has been unaccounted for since the day of the attack. Drama, starring LL Cool J

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: On a live-fire air force training exercise, someone hacks into the control systems of a drone and fires one of its two live missiles, striking and killing a serviceman, before piloting it away. Sam and Callen meet with a member of the National Security Agency, while Eric's inquiries lead him to a group of students on an advanced computer science course and Kensi is sent under cover to pose as its guest speaker

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: A man who stole a car and ran down and killed one of his pursuers before escaping is identified as Walton Flynn, an ex-marine now working for a private security firm in Iraq. Sam and Callen learn that the businessman he had been hired to protect had been murdered and that the people chasing him were hired by his employers to dish out their own form of justice. Crime drama, starring Chris O'Donnell and LL Cool J

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: A jet-skier crashes on to the shore and is discovered to be dead, having been shot through the chest. At HQ, Hetty shows the team surveillance footage of the shooting and identifies the victim as Daniel Su, an engineer who defected from North Korea and was working for the US government on a highly classified research project. Sam and Callen visit the victim's apartment, where they find the body of a woman on the floor

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Marine Brandon Valdivia falls to his death from a rooftop bar and is initially thought to have committed suicide, but his family refuse to accept that possibility and persuade a congressman to put some pressure on the admiral. Sam and Callen are then brought in to re-evaluate the case and discover that Valdivia may have been involved in money laundering with a fellow marine.

DiNozzo and McGee find links on Rosie's laptop to possible suspect John 'Diesel' Stevens

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles

programme.title: Lovejoy
programme.desc: The roguish antiques dealer is arrested on suspicion of armed robbery - and to make matters worse, the only way he can clear his name is by admitting he was with a charming ex-girlfriend at the time. He must decide whether to come clean and face a jealous Charlotte's wrath, or take the consequences of the charge. Ian McShane stars

programme.title: Lovejoy
programme.desc: A penniless sculptor seeks the canny dealer's help to prise a considerable inheritance out of his avaricious brother. Celia Imrie guest stars, with Ian McShane

programme.title: Lovejoy
programme.desc: The police suspect Lovejoy of stealing from one of Jane's old friends, forcing him to enlist the help of refuse collector extraordinaire Brian Nun to clear himself. Unfortunately, matters are complicated by Eric's 'bin diving' habit. Light-hearted drama, starring Ian McShane and Phyllis Logan

programme.title: Lovejoy
programme.desc: Two mysterious murders appear to be the work of an ancient Mayan curse attached to a statue acquired by Lovejoy. Drama, starring Ian McShane and Phyllis Logan

programme.title: Lovejoy
programme.desc: The dodgy dealer is asked to furnish a restaurant, but the items he acquires turn out to be stolen. To make matters worse, the owners turn up for the establishment's opening night. John Wells guest stars, with Ian McShane

programme.title: Sharpe
programme.desc: Richard Sharpe and the faithful Harper begin their latest tour of duty by uncovering top-level corruption in the Army. Reporting their findings leads to attempts on their lives, so they fake their own deaths and re-enlist under new identities in an attempt to expose the culprits. Guest starring Julian Fellowes and featuring Alexander Armstrong in an early role

programme.title: Rosemary & Thyme
programme.desc: Laura and Rosemary are hired to re-create a Victorian garden in Regent's Park, where they meet all manner of intriguing characters - but the oasis of calm in central London is shattered by the discovery of a body. The victim is a respected Harley Street psychiatrist - whose three-legged dog appears to hold the key to solving the case. Mystery drama, starring Felicity Kendal and Pam Ferris

programme.title: Rosemary & Thyme
programme.desc: The duo are hired to create a garden as a wedding present, and find a baby under a gooseberry bush. The discovery brings out Rosemary's maternal side as the pair investigate who abandoned the child. Their task is complicated by the murder of an artistic neighbour - and the two mysterious incidents appear to be connected. Starring Felicity Kendal and Pam Ferris

programme.title: Rosemary & Thyme
programme.desc: The duo visit a resort in Spain to create a trophy garden for a pro-am tennis tournament run by a former champion. When one of the competitors is found dead, everyone assumes it to be a gruesome accident - but there is more to the case than meets the eye. While investigating, Laura enters the mysterious world of bullfighting, and Rosemary rediscovers her love of motor scooters

programme.title: Rosemary & Thyme
programme.desc: The green-fingered amateur detectives work on a garden for an open-air theatre, where Rosemary's cousin is due to perform. However, the production suffers a setback when an actor shoots himself during rehearsal - and the gun is found to have contained live bullets instead of blanks. A member of the cast gets an attack of stage fright, and Laura is obliged to take her place in the play as the duo try to find the culprit. Drama, with Felicity Kendal and Pam Ferris

programme.title: The Brokenwood Mysteries
programme.desc: During a performance of Hamlet, young cast member Ben Faulkner collapses and dies. Initially the detectives think Ben died of natural causes, until Mike uncovers a complex web of ego-driven dissension among the troupe and follows his nose to the real cause of death

programme.title: Shakespeare & Hathaway - Private Investigators
programme.desc: Frank and Lu investigate a death threat at Runningbrook, an exclusive tennis club that hides a toxic threat. Crime drama, starring Mark Benton and Jo Joyner

programme.title: Rebus
programme.desc: While Rebus looks into the apparent suicide of a man at an investment bank, Clarke investigates a stabbing on the seedy Westmills estate. The inspector learns his victim had links to a chemical company building a new plant in the area, as well as an Angolan village where children have been used unscrupulously in drug testing - but before his latest informant can pass on vital evidence, she too is silenced. Drama, starring Ken Stott and Claire Price

programme.title: Wallander
programme.desc: Kenneth Branagh returns to his Bafta-winning role of the Swedish detective in three adaptations of stories by Henning Mankell. Wallander moves into a new home with his girlfriend, only for their dreams of domestic bliss to be shattered when they discover a skeleton in the garden. He must find the killer if he has any hopes of getting his life settled again - and is surprised to discover the body was buried more recently than he first thought. Saskia Reeves and Sarah Smart co-star

programme.title: Judge John Deed
programme.desc: Part one of two. Deed reluctantly helps Jo by examining why funding for legal aid was withdrawn in the case of a soldier who committed suicide after being damaged by army vaccines. However, he risks causing a constitutional crisis when he decides the only way to win justice for the deceased's family is to accuse the original judge of bias

programme.title: Judge John Deed
programme.desc: Part two of two. Deed's investigation into why a soldier's legal funding to sue a pharmaceutical company was withdrawn leads to more sinister discoveries. Forces begin to work against him with shocking consequences. Legal drama, starring Martin Shaw, Jenny Seagrove and Donald Sinden

programme.title: Life on Mars
programme.desc: A drug dealer dies while in police custody and Sam suspects a cover-up within the unit. He orders a full investigation, though it seems none of the other officers is prepared to help. To Gene's relief, the pathologist's report appears to put everyone in the clear, but when the truth about the criminal's demise emerges, it is up to the 21st-century cop to decide whether to reveal it. John Simm and Philip Glenister star

programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: First of a three-part story. Stuart encourages Stevie to again work undercover as heroin dealer Rob Towler's girlfriend, not realising the chemistry that still exists between them. The case progresses well, especially when Stevie's ex-boss Jim Rutherford turns up to help. Eventually her cover is blown by a member of the gang as the Sun Hill detectives close in for a sting operation, and Stuart is left questioning his colleague's loyalties when she whisks Towler away on her motorbike and disappears without trace. Continues on Monday

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part two of three. The evidence against undercover cop Stevie mounts up, suggesting she has thrown away her career for the love of a villain. Stuart finally catches up with the pair, and his suspicions are confirmed when she turns a gun on her colleague and makes her escape. With the violent Tameside Crew wreaking carnage across Sun Hill in pursuit of Towler, Stuart is suspended from duty for being too close to the case. Concludes tomorrow

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part three of three. In a flashback sequence, Stevie reveals to Stuart how she discovered the drug deal was a set-up and why she disobeyed instructions in order to protect her boyfriend. Stuart is astonished that her erratic behaviour was designed to save his life and expose the real villain behind the Tameside Crew. As she makes plans to flee the country, the gang seems always to be two steps ahead of her - and Stevie still has to make the biggest decision of her life

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Mel's younger brother and his girlfriend come to stay on New Year's Eve and she goes to work thinking the couple are happily celebrating in Trafalgar Square - until Sun Hill officers are called to investigate a stabbing in a club. With the troublesome youngster in the frame for the attack, apparently provoked by a fellow clubber who made advances to his companion, Mel sets out to persuade her colleagues he is innocent

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: Sharon agrees to play along with her wayward husband's hare-brained scheme to pay off his gambling debts, and soon gets cajoled into appearing on a TV game show and trying to track down a collectable car in Berlin. Meanwhile, Dorien leaves Marcus and moves into the girls' house next door. The 1990 Christmas special, starring Pauline Quirke, Linda Robson, Lesley Joseph and Peter Polycarpou

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Poplar is rocked by a series of violent attacks on women, and a prostitute is too ashamed to report what she knows - but her actions lead to a dangerous man roaming the streets, with devastating consequences for the residents of Nonnatus House. A mother tries to keep her daughter's pregnancy a secret to avoid gossip, putting her life in danger. The Turners go on a long overdue holiday, but the weather ruins their plans

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: The introduction of the contraceptive pill is welcomed by Dr Turner, but some of his colleagues are less keen on the idea. Patsy tries to help a bargee who wants to give birth among her own community rather than in a maternity ward. Nurse Crane deals with an emergency during a storm, and a familiar face returns to Nonnatus House - but has been changed by her time away

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Dr Turner is horrified to realise he may be partly to blame as the cause of the recent rise in birth deformities comes to light, and tries to prevent further cases. Sister Evangelina returns to work, but has clearly been affected badly by her stroke, and her health continues to deteriorate. A pregnant bride goes into labour during her wedding reception, while her mother is forced to come to terms with her grandchild being conceived out of wedlock

programme.title: Catherine Cookson's The Fifteen Streets
programme.desc: Feature-length drama based on Catherine Cookson's novel set in early 20th-century Newcastle. Worker John O'Brien lives near poverty in a small house in the workers district, yet falls in love with teacher Mary, daughter of a rich family. Despite the pressures exerted by the social difference, the vigorous Mary refuses to allow outer circumstances destroy their relationship. Starring Sean Bean and Clare Holman

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Sister Julienne helps a woman from Pakistan who is unable to have children, but who has found to her shock that her husband is bringing a second wife into their home, who is already eight months pregnant. Trixie hits the bottle following her break-up with Christopher, Dr Turner tries to persuade Sister Monica Joan to admit that her eyesight is failing, and Lucille helps a woman with a compulsion to eat coal

programme.title: Miss Marple
programme.desc: Feature-length episode of the mystery drama. The sleuthing senior citizen is urged by an old friend to visit her sister as she is convinced something is wrong. Miss Marple heads for the country to investigate and it is not long before a heated argument in a locked room ends with a gunshot that leaves a man dead. Jean Simmons and Joss Ackland guest star, with Joan Hickson

programme.title: Babs
programme.desc: Biopic of Barbara Windsor, following the key events of the actress' life over the course of 50 years. The story begins in 1993, with the actress preparing for a stage performance shortly before joining the cast of EastEnders. She looks back to her childhood as a lonely evacuee in the Second World War, her rise to fame in the Carry On films and her difficult relationships with her father and her first husband, gangster Ronnie Knight. Samantha Spiro, Jaime Winstone, Honor Kneafsey and Windsor herself share the lead role

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Poplar is rocked by a series of violent attacks on women, and a prostitute is too ashamed to report what she knows - but her actions lead to a dangerous man roaming the streets, with devastating consequences for the residents of Nonnatus House. A mother tries to keep her daughter's pregnancy a secret to avoid gossip, putting her life in danger. The Turners go on a long overdue holiday, but the weather ruins their plans

programme.title: Lovejoy
programme.desc: The antiques dealer's visiting American cousin flees the UK with the loot from a raid on Wakering Hall. Feature-length Christmas special from 1993, guest starring John Gielgud and Ken Kercheval

programme.title: Where the Heart Is
programme.desc: Dick severely injures a boy in a car accident, filling him with guilt and remorse, while Peggy faces up to a painful problem of her own. Drama, starring Pam Ferris, Sarah Lancashire and William Travis

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: When a young mother is found badly beaten in an alleyway, Sun Hill CID is drawn into her tale of debt, blackmail and a complex love life. The victim was seen arguing with a former boyfriend in a nightclub the previous day, and DNA evidence recovered from under her fingernails leads to a man who she owed a huge amount of money to. Christopher Timothy and Duncan James guest star

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Oliver eagerly anticipates the award ceremony, where he and Jac may be honoured for their research. However, the arrival of a patient with an unusual condition forces him to choose between his moment of glory and carrying out an operation. Chantelle thinks Lleucu has ruined her Valentine's Day plans, while Sacha discovers he may not be able to give Chrissie everything he had intended

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Dixie is upset when Cyd confronts her about recommending Greg for a transfer and considers resigning herself. Nathan's attempts to talk Selena out of having an abortion are thwarted by a leaking sluice pipe and his day takes a turn for the worse when he makes a shocking discovery about Stitch. Jane Hazlegrove and Ben Price star

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part one of two. A couple return from a night away to discover their baby has been abducted from her bed. The child's nanny - the last person to see her - gives a garbled account of events, covering for her boyfriend who had visited on the night in question. But as Tony gently interrogates the parents, another suspect emerges

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Irene is still annoying Simon. Bianca doesn't want Diane and Jacques staying with her anymore. Michael and Matthew move into the Square

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Terry moves into the Vic. Dixon confesses his affair with Jamie to George.

programme.title: A Place to Call Home
programme.desc: Sarah leads the charge to rescue James, whose ordeal continues when his sexuality becomes known, and puts her relationship with George at risk when a disturbing discovery forces her to reveal her darkest secret

programme.title: All Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: Tristan appears to have an ulterior motive for taking an interest in Tricky Woo's diet, and Mrs Pumphrey gets James to be the judge at the vegetable show. Rural drama, starring Christopher Timothy and Peter Davison

programme.title: Dangerfield
programme.desc: Alison decides she can manage on her own and rents a remote country cottage, but the discovery that there's an arsonist at large soon has Paul fearing for her safety. Drama, starring Nigel Le Vaillant, Tamzin Malleson and Tim Vincent

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: Sharon is convinced her 30th birthday is just the start of another three decades of misery, prompting Tracey to question the wisdom of throwing a party. Pauline Quirke and Linda Robson star

programme.title: Hi-de-Hi!
programme.desc: A tattered shoebox filled with money arrives for Jeffrey, with instructions from Joe Maplin that it be used to bribe local councillors to not build a hospital next to the holiday camp. Comedy, starring Paul Shane

programme.title: Keeping Up Appearances
programme.desc: Hyacinth attends an exclusive auction, while Daisy and Rose try to cool the ardour of a woman who seems determined to marry Daddy. Comedy, starring Patricia Routledge, Judy Cornwell and Josephine Tewson

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Howard employs Clegg to take care of several suspect picnic objects, but one item receives his refusal and Howard will not look after it until Pearl is out of sight. Comedy, starring Peter Sallis and Robert Fyfe

programme.title: The Coroner
programme.desc: Jane and Davey investigate a skydiving accident and discover that someone had tampered with the victim's parachute. Crime drama, starring Claire Goose

programme.title: Luther
programme.desc: A twisted fetishist is murdering women in a horrific echo of an unsolved case from the 1980s. Luther is keen that he and his team concentrate all their resources into catching the copycat before he or she strikes again, only for his focus to be divided by a second case involving a malicious internet tormentor found dead in his home. Pulled in every direction, the cop's patience begins to wear thin, so the last thing he needs is to deal with a road accident - until he sets eyes on one of the women involved. Is he finally on the verge of finding love? Crime drama, starring Idris Elba, cwith Warren Brown, Sienna Guillory, Nikki Amuka-Bird and Dermot Crowley

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: Brian finds the library card of cold case victim Dr Richard Symes, leading the team to reinvestigate the death of the professor, who perished after falling from the roof of his college three years' previously. Sandra and Gerry interview Symes' widow, who believes principal Jeremy Ventham drove her husband to suicide following a conflict over teaching methods. Crime drama, starring Alun Armstrong and Amanda Redman

programme.title: Trial & Retribution
programme.desc: DI Pat North is assigned to look into a case that may clear a man convicted of murder eight years ago, and finds that DS Walker was in charge of the original inquiry. Drama, starring Kate Buffery, David Hayman and James Wilby

programme.title: The Casual Vacancy
programme.desc: Drama adapted from the novel by JK Rowling, set in an English village whose seemingly idyllic facade hides a community at war. At the parish council meeting, chairman Howard Mollison introduces his plans to turn the community centre into a health spa, but he reckons without local solicitor Barry Fairbrother, who makes an impassioned speech about the importance of the centre to the impoverished people of the nearby estate. However, an unexpected turn of events leaves an empty seat on the council - leading to the biggest battle Pagford has ever seen. Michael Gambon, Rory Kinnear, Keeley Hawes and Julia McKenzie star

programme.title: Life on Mars
programme.desc: The investigation into the murder of a bookies' clerk leads the team to 29-year-old Vic Tyler - who Sam is shocked to discover is his own father. He realises the time when he and his mother were abandoned is coming up and desperately tries to persuade the small-time gambler not to leave his wife and child. Meanwhile, Gene sees an opportunity to bring down a crime syndicate

programme.title: Our Food, Our Family with Michela Chiappa
programme.desc: The Welsh-Italian cook meets four generations of food lovers in Cardiff. While joining the Aslams to rustle up some of their favourite curries, fish dishes and a keema bolognese, Michela meets 90-year-old head of the family Mohammed, who came from Bangladesh in 1958 and went on to open one of Wales' very first Asian restaurants. Michela tries to prise the family's secret spice mix as well as helping grandson Yusuf to master one of his grandmother's specialities to bring along to a special party to celebrate his grandparents' 54th wedding anniversary

programme.title: Tennis: Eastbourne
programme.desc: The Viking International Eastbourne. Clare Balding presents coverage of the women's singles final on day five from Devonshire Park Lawn Tennis Club. Karolina Pliskova of the Czech Republic took the honours when this tournament was last staged in 2019, prevailing 6-1, 6-4 against Germany's 2018 Wimbledon champion Angelique Kerber in the showpiece match

programme.title: MOTD Live: UEFA Euro 2020
programme.desc: Wales v Denmark (Kick-off 5.00pm). Coverage of the opening match in the last 16, which takes place at Johan Cruijff ArenA in Amsterdam. Wales only won one of their group matches, but that victory against Turkey and their draw against Switzerland was enough to see them finish second behind Italy, who romped through the group stage, winning all three matches without conceding a goal. Denmark seemed to be heading for an early exit after losing their opening two fixtures against Finland and Belgium, but a remarkable 4-1 thumping of Russia, and other results going their way, saw them progress as Group B runners-up

programme.title: MOTD Live: UEFA Euro 2020
programme.desc: Italy v Austria (Kick-off 8.00pm). All the action from the second last-16 fixture, which takes place at Wembley Stadium. The Italians have been one of the outstanding teams in the tournament so far, moving from dark horses to being one of the favourites to lift the trophy. They defeated both Turkey and Switzerland 3-0, before dominating Wales in a 1-0 victory to stretch their winning record to 10 consecutive matches, all of which have been without conceding a single goal. Austria finished second behind the Netherlands in Group C, defeating North Macedonia and Ukraine to progress beyond the opening group phase of a major tournament for the first time since 1982

programme.title: Planet Earth

The film ranges from the East Cape of South Africa to the north of Norway and from Chile to the Indian Ocean to show how animals overcome adversity to survive and thrive in some of the world's most challenging environments

programme.desc: Eric Knowles hosts the show from Norfolk's biggest Antique Centre in King's Lynn. Experts Caroline Hawley and Mark Stacey assist the Reds and the Blues as they do battle

programme.title: Wimbledon 2021
programme.desc: Sue Barker presents coverage of the opening day from the All England Club, as the first round continues in the men's and ladies' singles competitions. There was a major upset on Centre Court on day one when the tournament was last held in 2019, with Yulia Putintseva defeating second seed Naomi Osaka 7-6, 6-2. This was followed by a straight-sets victory for home favourite Kyle Edmund over Jaume Munar, and the London resident will hope the support of the fans can help him to go far this time around. Elsewhere, Karolina Pliskova followed up her triumph at Eastbourne with a 6-2, 7-6 victory over Zhu Lin, and Coco Gauff demonstrated her enormous potential when she prevailed 6-4, 6-4 against five-time champion Venus Williams at the age of just 15

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Bernie tells Tiffany the weight-loss pills are not working fast enough and she needs stronger ones but her friend refuses. Vi encourages Stuart and Rainie to find a distraction from Abi, prompting them to make a decision about the surrogacy. Rocky has an idea to bring Mila and Iqra back together, while Isaac tells Lola he has a surprise for her

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Tiffany reluctantly gives more pills to Bernie, who is disappointed to discover she has not lost as much weight as she had hoped - convincing her she has blown her chance of becoming Rainie's surrogate. Isaac pays a visit to Ruby at the club - and he has plenty to say - and Kathy helps to clear the air between Mila and Iqra

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Lucky tries to overcome her personal crisis by throwing herself into work. But in her eagerness to go above and beyond she oversteps the mark. A patient accuses Dominic of leaving an unwanted item in his body during an operation, and Kylie put her foot in it with her flatmates

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and the Rich Brothers compete to design an Ibiza-themed party garden for a family in Wokingham, Berkshire. All concerned hope to come up with some cool ideas to transform this unloved plot into a laid-back Balearic chill out lounge on a budget of £3,000

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: Olivia Colman finds some surprises in her ancestral history, and her belief that most of those in her family tree emanate from Norfolk turns out to be far from the truth. Olivia is astonished to discover that she needs to travel to India to find out more about her great-great-great grandmother Harriot, who was an orphan in 1811 on a ship bound for England from Calcutta. Harriot's father was an an Englishman, but the identity of her mother, who gave birth to her in a remote Indian village, is a mystery Olivia delights in solving

programme.title: Do Black Lives Still Matter?
programme.desc: Grime artist Saskilla concludes the series with a look at ongoing issues with diversity in the music industry, questioning the long-term benefits of the Blackout Tuesday social media campaign and if the money pledged by major labels is enough to bring about lasting change

programme.title: UEFA Euro 2020 Highlights
programme.desc: Action from the fifth and sixth last-16 matches, which were Croatia v Spain at Parken in Copenhagen and France v Switzerland at National Arena Bucharest

programme.title: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programme.desc: Citizen Khan star Adil Ray hosts an extended edition of the satirical quiz, with comedian Kiri Pritchard-McLean and Conservative MP Nicky Morgan joining team captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the week's events

programme.title: MOTD: UEFA Euro 2020 Replay
programme.desc: France v Switzerland. A chance to see the sixth match in the last 16, which took place at National Arena Bucharest

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Wild & Weird
programme.desc: Naomi Wilkinson and Tim Warwood investigate gruesome goings on, including a strange slime invading people's gardens, and a plant that pretends to be a dead animal

programme.title: The Dengineers
programme.desc: Rhodri, 10, is mad about cricket but is unable to go to his local club as often as he would like, so needs a cricket den where he can practice whenever he likes

programme.title: The Dog Ate My Homework
programme.desc: Team captains Orson and Janae battle it with the help of comedians Thanyia Moore and Helen Bauer, Officially Amazing's Ben Shires and Dr Ranj Singh

programme.title: Blue Peter
programme.desc: Richie and Mwaksy take on an army challenge

programme.title: What's Cooking Omari?
programme.desc: Brothers Omari and Laquarn make a banana shake breakfast shaky shake shake. This delicious breakfast is the perfect start for a busy day

programme.title: Deadly Dinosaurs with Steve Backshall
programme.desc: Steve is on a mission to find the weirdest dinosaur ever, starting with gigantoraptor, an eight-metre-tall giant that laid the biggest eggs in history

programme.title: Human Universe
programme.desc: On a trip to the fortified Moroccan village of Ait-Ben-Haddou in the Atlas Mountains, Professor Brian Cox reveals how by watching the stars' motion across the night sky, it is quite natural for man to think he is at the centre of everything. That view was held for many ages, but innate human curiosity has eventually led to an understanding of mankind's true place in space and time, and an appreciation that Earth is not a focal point but a mere particle of rock in a possibly infinite expanse of space, 13.8 billion years from the beginning of the universe

programme.title: The Outlaw (1943)
programme.desc: Notorious gunslinger Doc Holliday is reunited with his lawman friend Pat Garrett as he goes in search of his stolen horse. When he finds the thief is the infamous outlaw Billy the Kid, tensions grow between the three men, and their relationships with the same woman only make things worse. Howard Hughes' Western, starring Jane Russell, Walter Huston, Jack Buetel and Thomas Mitchell

programme.title: Mary Berry's Country House Secrets
programme.desc: Mary visits Powderham Castle in Devon to spend time with the Earl and Countess of Devon and their two young children, who are reinventing the Earldom for the 21st century. Mary's time with the family reveals stories of romance and tragedy as she discovers more of this extraordinary home. In the castle's historic kitchen, Mary prepares a Devon cream tea, before making a delicate peach posset for a Midsummer Eve woodland party the family are throwing to celebrate their two year anniversary at the castle

programme.title: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: A retrospective look at the television appearances made over the years by actor Jack Lemmon, capturing the highlights and milestones of his life and career. Narrated by actress Sylvia Syms

programme.title: Life in the Air
programme.desc: Wildlife series following animals that are able to defy gravity in various ways. The first programme features caracal cats, which jump to catch birds out of the air, and reveals how kangaroos' hopping movements help them find water in the desert. Leaping snakes are able to glide without wings in the jungles of Borneo while insects are able to accelerate up to 700G

programme.title: Brian Cox's Adventures in Space and Time
programme.desc: Brian explores the enigma of time, a phenomenon that is taken for granted but which, as far as physicists are concerned, is one of the biggest mysteries in the universe. He recalls highlights from his TV series that touch upon different sides of this physics conundrum, from breaking the sound barrier in a Eurofighter in order to see two sunsets in one day, to witnessing one of the oldest animal life cycles on Earth on a remote beach in Costa Rica

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Memorable moments and previously unseen finds, including a handkerchief commemorating cricket legend Dr WG Grace and three postcard albums found on a council tip

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: In Stockholm, Michael Portillo braves a precarious tour of the city from its rooftops, before boarding a heritage tram to get the lowdown on 1930s Sweden from an expert

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo travels from Stockholm into the Arctic Circle, along the way decorating a Dala horse and learning how Sweden created a welfare state

programme.title: Casablanca (1942)
programme.desc: The American expat owner of a Moroccan nightclub is reunited with his old flame during the Second World War. Now married to a Czech freedom fighter, she needs his help to get them safely out of the country before they are captured by the Nazis - but the former lovers also have to wrestle the temptation to rekindle their simmering romance. Drama, with Humphrey Bogart, Ingrid Bergman, Claude Rains and Paul Henreid

programme.title: T20 Cricket
programme.desc: England v Sri Lanka. Action from the third and final T20 of the series, taking place at Ageas Bowl in Southampton. The sides last met here in this format in July 2016, when Jos Buttler's innings of 73 not out helped England to an eight-wicket triumph

programme.title: Eric and Ernie - Behind the Scenes
programme.desc: Victoria Wood narrates the comedy duo's early years, considering why they remain Britain's best-loved double act. Archive footage and celebrity interviews combine to reveal the hard work that went into Morecambe and Wise's act and the secrets of their phenomenal success. Contributors include scriptwriter Eddie Braben, their widows Doreen and Joan, Cilla Black, Michael Grade, Miranda Hart, Penelope Keith and Andrew Marr

programme.title: Glastonbury in the 21st Century
programme.desc: Dizzee Rascal narrates a look at the festival since the turn of the century, with appearances by emerging global headliners such as Coldplay and the Killers, and a wildly packed Chemical Brothers performance from 2000. Plus, the much-debated arrival of hip-hop with Jay-Z in 2008, Beyoncé's game-changing performance in 2011, Kylie Minogue's return and Stormzy's defining headline set in 2019, as well as the Sunday afternoon Legend slots

programme.title: Glastonbury 2019
programme.desc: Lauren Laverne and Clara Amfo introduce Kylie Minogue's performance on the Pyramid Stage. This set marked the singer's first appearance at the festival, after being forced to pull out for health reasons in 2005, and featured hits from throughout her 30-year career. Plus, highlights from Miley Cyrus and Koffee's performances, and a film introduced by the Rev Kate Bottley exploring the spiritual history of Glastonbury

programme.title: Arctic Monkeys at Glastonbury 2013
programme.desc: The band's headline set on the Pyramid stage on the Friday night of the festival, featuring performances of songs including Do I Wanna Know?, I Bet You Look Good on the Dancefloor, Fake Tales of San Francisco and When the Sun Goes Down

programme.title: Captain Fantastic (2016)
programme.desc: A father raises his six children off the grid in the forests of the Pacific Northwest, creating a rigorous physical and intellectual process of education for them. He faces the challenge of reintegrating his family into society, a task that forces him to reassess his idea of what it means to be a parent. Comedy drama, starring Viggo Mortensen and Frank Langella

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Life in a Cottage Garden with Carol Klein
programme.desc: Autumn brings a change of mood to the garden and it is a busy time for the horticulturist - there are seedlings to be pricked out, tidying up needs to be done and harvest time means there is an abundance of seed to collect. Slugs and snails have ravaged the hot borders and Carol cuts back tattered foliage to reduce the damage just in time to open Glebe Cottage to the public under the National Gardens Scheme

programme.title: Britain in Bloom
programme.desc: Chris Bavin heads to northern Lincolnshire to meet the volunteers from Immingham in Bloom. They've been taking part in Britain In Bloom for seven years and have won gold before. However, this time the group is upping the ante by taking a huge risk. Chris finds out how they plan to retain their crown by including the port itself in their entry, with plans to brighten this enormous grey industrial area with colourful flowers

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Adam Frost offers tips on laying turf, and planting succulents. He also adds perennial plants to his kitchen garden to grow as cut flowers. Plus, Joe Swift travels to Marwood Hill Gardens in Devon to explore plants that thrive in moist conditions

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Ellie Harrison is in Gloucestershire at Nature in Art, the world's first museum and art gallery dedicated to art inspired by nature, as she goes in search of her inner muse. While picking up a few artistic tips, Ellie introduces a selection of stories from the archives that demonstrate how the countryside has been an abiding influence, from early man to modern-day artists braving the extremes to capture a landscape or using wildlife itself to create a picture

programme.title: Landward
programme.desc: Dougie Vipond visits the Royal Highland Show, where he meets competitors who have travelled to Ingliston to show off their animals to a worldwide, digital audience. Plus, a look at conservationist and writer Polly Pullar's efforts to rehabilitate red squirrels

programme.title: Beechgrove
programme.desc: Brian Cunningham and Carole Baxter are being neighbourly in Beechgrove as they swap stories over the garden fence between Brian's Alpine garden and Carole's seaside garden. Plus, a catch-up with Sophie McKilligan in her new role as gardener at Culzean Castle in Ayrshire

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Best Bites
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt presents highlights from throughout the series, offering viewers a chance to revisit some of their favourite moments from previous years in the kitchen

programme.title: Nadiya's Summer Feasts
programme.desc: Nadiya Hussain prepares an array of mouth-watering dishes that she will serve up to her loved ones at her own summer party feast, including a barbecued butterflied leg of lamb with sticky rhubarb glaze. She makes side dishes of kiwi and feta salad and edamame wild rice, as well as olive and rosemary bread shaped like a crown. For dessert, she goes all out with a magical princess torte cake and an ingenious raspberry ripple eclair, and creates a thirst-quenching drink in the form of an elderflower cooler

programme.title: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: Sylvia Syms narrates a history of Westerns, featuring archive interviews with stars including Gary Cooper, James Stewart, Yul Brynner, Paul Newman, John Wayne and Clint Eastwood

programme.title: Paint Your Wagon (1969)
programme.desc: A gold prospector buys a wife from a Mormon and agrees to share her with his mining partner when she insists they all live together. Mindful that her presence in an all-male town might give rise to jealousy, he hijacks a stagecoach carrying six working girls and opens a brothel in town. Oscar-nominated musical Western, starring Lee Marvin, Clint Eastwood, with Jean Seberg and Harve Presnell

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: At Dover Castle in Kent, Jonathan Pratt and James Lewis scour the crowd to find objects to take off to auction. Jonathan discovers an elegant Lalique figure and James finds a pristine collection of Chinese snuff bottles. Presenter Paul Martin uncovers the murderous history of the castle

programme.title: Our Wild Adventures
programme.desc: BBC wildlife presenters look back on trips across the continent, with Steve Backshall reflecting on being dropped into the nest of a wild saltwater crocodile to help scientists in the Northern Territory to assess the species' health. Gordon Buchanan reveals how faced his own fear of sharks when he visited Owarigi in the Solomon Islands, and Liz Bonnin looks back on witnessing the hatching of baby green sea turtles on Heron Island on the Great Barrier Reef,

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: Raymond and a team of volunteers give the valais blacknose sheep a bath, Kirsty and Tom rescue fallen sheep on the isle, and Stevie attempts to milk his first buffalo. In Shetland, Kirsty and Tom again row out to St Ninian's Isle to attempt to rescue a ewe and a lamb that have fallen from the isle's steep cliffs

programme.title: Africa with Ade Adepitan
programme.desc: Ade explores Tanzania, Ethiopia, and Somalia, visiting a game reserve where efforts are being made to halt the decline of elephant numbers. He tours Addis Ababa with former Olympic champion Haile Gebrselassie to discover why Ethiopia's economy has gone from strength to strength in recent years, and discovers the impact of war on Somalia, meeting a former NHS doctor who has returned to the country to help rebuild

programme.title: Legends of Glastonbury
programme.desc: Lauren Laverne introduces memorable performances from the festival over the years, including appearances by Paul McCartney, Paul Simon, Kylie Minogue, Madness, Ladysmith Black Mambazo, Crosby, Stills & Nash, Jimmy Cliff and Patti Smith. Also featured are BB King, Pretenders, Status Quo, Blondie, Eddy Grant, Shirley Bassey and the Who

programme.title: Live at Worthy Farm
programme.desc: Jo Whiley presents a behind-the-scenes documentary following director Paul Dugdale and his team as they put together this year's streamed event. Featuring interviews with organiser Emily Eavis and key creative talent, as well as artists who performed, including Damon Albarn, Kano, Roisin Murphy, Wolf Alice, Kae Tempest and Coldplay

programme.title: Live at Worthy Farm
programme.desc: A selection of performances from the event streamed across the globe in May, shot by Grammy-nominated director Paul Dugdale at Worthy Farm. Artists featured include Coldplay, Damon Albarn, Haim, Idles, Jorja Smith, Kano, Wolf Alice, Michael Kiwanuka and Roisin Murphy, while spoken-word elements are provided by guests including PJ Harvey, Jarvis Cocker, Kae Tempest, George The Poet and Michael Eavis

programme.title: Anita & Me (2002)
programme.desc: Twelve-year-old Meena strikes up an unlikely friendship with blonde teenage tearaway Anita. Despite their ethnic and social differences, the girls manage to support each other through difficult times while their families cope with life's problems. Comedy drama set in a Midlands village in the 1970s and based on the novel by Meera Syal, starring Chandeep Uppal, Anna Brewster, Max Beesley, Sanjeev Bhaskar, Kathy Burke, Lynn Redgrave and Ayesha Dharker

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce introduces the weekly topical debate, with a panel of politicians and other guests facing questions from an audience made up of members of the general public

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Fletch's relationship with Evie grows strained as decisions are made about Andrei's future, and he finds himself forced to break her heart to protect his daughter. Ange and Josh try to avoid each other, but must learn to put personal issues aside when a patient's life hangs in the balance. Overwhelmed with guilt, Jeong confesses his mistake with the drugs cupboard to Dom

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Charlie Ross looks at buying and selling design classics of the past, looking back at some of the best buys from past programmes. As well as revisiting some of the show's favourite experts and auctioneers, Charlie recalls a visit to the Brighton Pavilion where he learned all about its connection with a famous hat designer

programme.title: Unbeatable
programme.desc: Over three rounds, contestants John, Maria, Caroline and Mark vie to find the single 'unbeatable' answer and streak ahead of the rest to make the final where they can win a cash prize. General knowledge quiz, hosted by Jason Manford

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Optics expert Richard Biggs works on a home-made telescope, built by the owner's grandfather from bits of scrap during the Second World War. Saddler Suzie Fletcher is brought a leather briefcase once owned by an intrepid traveller, while furniture restorer Will Kirk works his magic on a vintage writing bureau. The final challenge sees Dominic Chinea reviving a rusty child's seesaw that dates from the 1930s and was ahead of its time - not only did it go up and down, but it also spun round. Can Dominic get it spinning again?

programme.title: The Week in Parliament
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's proceedings in Parliament, presented by David Cornock

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: The latest stories from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Wimbledon 2021
programme.desc: Sue Barker presents coverage of the opening day from the All England Club, featuring the first round of the men's and ladies' singles competitions, including Novak Djokovic v Jack Draper. The tournament was cancelled last year for the first time since the Second World War due to the Covid-19 pandemic, but it is expected to be business as usual this time around. As the defending men's champion, Djokovic will get proceedings under way on Centre Court, as he did in 2019, when he recorded a 6-3, 7-5, 6-3 victory over Philipp Kohlschreiber. The Serbian will be entering this competition on a high after recently being crowned French Open champion, ending the dominance of Rafael Nadal with a stunning four-set victory in the semi-finals, before coming from two sets down to defeat Stefanos Tsitsipas in the final, and it would be one of the biggest upsets in Wimbledon history should he fail at the first hurdle against British wild card Draper. After coming so close to his maiden Grand Slam title,

programme.title: Today at Wimbledon
programme.desc: Clare Balding presents highlights of the opening day's play at the All England Club, where the first round got under way in the men's and ladies' singles. Novak Djokovic was scheduled on Centre Court to begin the defence of the title he won when the tournament was last held in 2019

programme.title: Victoria Wood: The Secret List
programme.desc: With a career spanning more than 40 years and crossing every possible genre, Victoria Wood amassed a back catalogue bulging with sublime comedy moments. The first of two documentaries offers a unique opportunity to see the very best bits of Victoria's work that she loved the most, drawing on some of the 20 sketches chosen by her in a notebook discovered after her death. Plus, contributors including Russell T Davies, Ken Loach, Winsome Pinnock, Jane Wymark and Joan Armatrading discuss Victoria's broad catalogue of award-winning comedy. Narrated by Sara Pascoe

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, with Emily Maitlis

programme.title: Glastonbury in the 90s
programme.desc: The story of Glastonbury in the 1990s, arguably the festival's redefining decade. It was the era television cameras came, dance music conquered and the festival changed beyond all recognition. Told through archival material from the time and by those who were there, it's voiced by Skin from Skunk Anansie, who were the last headliners of the decade

programme.title: Glastonbury in the 21st Century
programme.desc: Dizzee Rascal narrates a look at the festival since the turn of the century, with appearances by emerging global headliners such as Coldplay and the Killers, and a wildly packed Chemical Brothers performance from 2000. Plus, the much-debated arrival of hip-hop with Jay-Z in 2008, Beyoncé's game-changing performance in 2011, Kylie Minogue's return and Stormzy's defining headline set in 2019, as well as the Sunday afternoon Legend slots

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Ellie Harrison presents an archive edition Nature in Art in Gloucestershire, the world's first museum and art gallery dedicated to art inspired by nature. While picking up a few artistic tips, Ellie introduces a selection of stories from the archives that demonstrate how the countryside has been an abiding influence, from early man to modern-day artists braving the extremes to capture a landscape or using wildlife itself to create a picture

programme.title: Hospital
programme.desc: University Hospitals Coventry and Warwickshire NHS Trust assess the aftermath of the pandemic and its long-term impact, and with the plastic surgery department finally able to start elective operations again, difficult decisions must be made over the investment in robotic surgery

programme.title: The Sheriffs Are Coming
programme.desc: Enforcement officers James and Mark help an 85-year-old man who is owed thousands by a roofing company who failed to pay the money the courts awarded

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: A Year in the Wild
programme.desc: A tour of Britain's largest national park, a land of extremes in the heart of the Highlands, where a diverse range of wildlife including golden eagles, ptarmigan, capercaillie, red deer and crested tits can be found. The programme explores the area through the eyes of people who live and work there, including a mountain guide, an artist, a gamekeeper and a nature writer. Narrated by Hermione Norris

programme.title: Michael Palin's New Europe
programme.desc: The veteran traveller completes his journey around eastern Europe. He learns how to skin a pig and make sausages in the Tatra mountains of Slovakia, enjoys a mud bath with the 2006 Miss World Tatana Kucharova in the spa town of Karlovy Vary and visits Turba's famous mime school in the Czech Republic. Finally, he travels to the holiday complex at Prora on the island of Rug, Germany, which was built by Hitler for his 'Strength Through Joy' programme

programme.title: Best of Glastonbury 2003
programme.desc: Highlights of the 2003 three-day festival, which featured performances by the Manic Street Preachers, REM, Radiohead, Macy Gray, Sugababes, Feeder, Echo and the Bunnymen and Fatboy Slim

programme.title: Iggy Pop and the Stooges at Glastonbury 2007
programme.desc: Performance by the veteran rockers at the famous music festival. Featuring tracks from their latest record The Weirdness and classic songs such as No Fun and I Wanna Be Your Dog

programme.title: Glastonbury 2017
programme.desc: Jo Whiley and Mark Radcliffe introduce highlights from the festival at Worthy Farm in Somerset, where Radiohead, Foo Fighters and Ed Sheeran were the headliners, including a reminder of some of the big performances, stories and moments from the weekend's events.

programme.title: The Killing II
programme.desc: Lund remains determined to pursue her target, despite Brix's orders. Buch's bold plan to respond to his predecessor's actions is thrown off course by a journalist's macabre revelation about the suppressed military investigation, while Raben tracks Louise - unaware Special Branch is keeping a close watch on him. Crime drama, starring Sofie Grabol. In Danish

programme.title: The Killing II
programme.desc: Raben's accusations leave Brix and Lund uncertain about whether their fellow officers can be trusted. Buch is forced to contemplate giving in to the prime minister's demands after his attack on the minister of defence backfires, while the focus of the investigation shifts once more. In Danish

programme.title: Discovery Concerts
programme.desc: Katie Derham introduces a performance of Benjamin Britten's The Young Person's Guide to the Orchestra by the BBC National Orchestra of Wales, conducted by Moritz Gnann. The concert is followed by an examination of the piece in which musicians discuss how the composer took classical techniques and reinvented them to create an optimistic vision for post-war Britain

programme.title: Maps: Power, Plunder and Possession
programme.desc: Professor Jerry Brotton examines the way maps have reflected contemporary politics and belief - and in some cases inspired them. He studies medieval religious cartography on maps showing pilgrims the routes to Jerusalem or heaven, Victorian illustrations of the world - with every nation awarded a score according to how 'civilised' they were deemed to be - and modern mapping of social problems including infant mortality and HIV

programme.title: World War Two: Behind Closed Doors
programme.desc: Laurence Rees investigates the events of 1941 when, having been betrayed by Hitler, Stalin switched his allegiance to Britain and America. It was an alliance based on military pragmatism which never altered the mutual antipathy between Churchill, who despised only Nazism above Communism, and the Soviet leader who knew the Red Army was ill equipped to cope with the Nazi onslaught alone

programme.title: Fela Kuti at Glastonbury 1984
programme.desc: Another chance to see the afrobeat pioneer's performance at the 1984 festival

programme.title: I Am A Mutoid: A Glastonbury Hero
programme.desc: Profile of Joe Rush, Glastonbury's 'waste alchemist' artist, and his underground travelling collective of subversive performers, musicians and artists. Featuring unseen archive from the Glastonbury Festival, the warehouse party scene, and art installations from London to Berlin

programme.title: Handmade in Japan
programme.desc: The production of the traditional garment, following the painstaking process of the silk being bound, hand-dyed, woven and finally turned into a kimono by a seamstress

programme.title: Discovery Concerts
programme.desc: Katie Derham introduces a performance of Benjamin Britten's The Young Person's Guide to the Orchestra by the BBC National Orchestra of Wales, conducted by Moritz Gnann. The concert is followed by an examination of the piece in which musicians discuss how the composer took classical techniques and reinvented them to create an optimistic vision for post-war Britain

programme.title: Maps: Power, Plunder and Possession
programme.desc: Professor Jerry Brotton examines the way maps have reflected contemporary politics and belief - and in some cases inspired them. He studies medieval religious cartography on maps showing pilgrims the routes to Jerusalem or heaven, Victorian illustrations of the world - with every nation awarded a score according to how 'civilised' they were deemed to be - and modern mapping of social problems including infant mortality and HIV

programme.title: A Year in the Wild
programme.desc: A tour of Britain's largest national park, a land of extremes in the heart of the Highlands, where a diverse range of wildlife including golden eagles, ptarmigan, capercaillie, red deer and crested tits can be found. The programme explores the area through the eyes of people who live and work there, including a mountain guide, an artist, a gamekeeper and a nature writer. Narrated by Hermione Norris

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross offers tips and tricks as he paints a faraway mountain nestled in misty clouds and foothills, creating the scene using soft, brilliant colours

programme.title: The Cruise
programme.desc: Champagne and caviar flow non-stop in the ship's casino, where a professional gambler plans to clean up and a couple of married croupiers aim to earn enough to buy a new home. Meanwhile, psychic songstress Jane tells fortunes all round

programme.title: Art of Persia
programme.desc: Broadcaster and journalist Samira Ahmed takes viewers on a remarkable journey to places rarely seen as she travels through Iran to tell the story of its complex and fascinating people, culture and history. She begins by visiting places rarely seen by Western audiences to reveal how the Persian empire was born, the mysterious writing of its early people and how its powerful kings built a vast realm that became the envy of the ancient world

programme.title: Imagine
programme.desc: Alan Yentob speaks to David Hockney about his new work at Westminster Abbey - a towering stained-glass window commissioned to celebrate 65 years since Queen Elizabeth II's coronation there. The artist famously refused a knighthood and declined an invitation to paint the Queen's portrait, so the announcement came as something of a surprising move from an artist whose career has continually defied expectation

programme.title: Face to Face
programme.desc: An archive interview with artist David Hockney

programme.title: Bob Ross: The Happy Painter
programme.desc: Documentary taking a behind-the-scenes look at American painter Bob Ross's journey from humble beginnings to pop culture icon, charting how one local TV commercial evolved into a viral phenomenon that continues to inspire millions around the world. The film reveals the public and private sides of Bob Ross through accounts from close friends and family, childhood photographs and rare archive footage. Interviewees recount his gentle, mild-mannered demeanour and unwavering dedication to wildlife as well as disclosing little-known facts about his famous curly hair - which was naturally straight - and his fascination with fast cars

programme.title: Motherland
programme.desc: Comedy about Julia, a middle-class mother who moves out of her comfort zone and reluctantly invites the entire class to her kid's birthday party. With her mum still refusing to help out, she relies on Liz's party hacks and Kevin's entertaining skills. Starring Anna Maxwell-Martin and Lucy Punch

programme.title: Motherland
programme.desc: A school fundraiser is not Julia's idea of an evening out but somehow she finds herself heavily involved in order to impress a former colleague and new mum crush. programme.title: Would You Rather
programme.desc: Would You Rather? is a brand new series for CBBC hosted by comedian Darren Harriott. In this episode, Darren is joined by social media sensations Adam Beales and Joe Tasker, alongside his usual crew of 15 kids, as they face some big and bamboozling dilemmas to debate. Our celebrity mates Adam and Joe are tasked with trying to think just like Darren’s Crew and guess which way they vote on a series of hilarious Would You Rather? dilemmas. This episode features fun dilemmas, such as Would You Rather… Have ears the size of an elephant or a trunk instead of a nose? Have hair that changes colour depending on the temperature or have eyes that change colour depending on your mood? Time Travel into the past or time travel into the future? Tune in to hear Adam and Joe’s hilarious answers, as well as the Crew’s amusing insights.
