programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: The first host in south Wales, super-confident 39-year-old cleaning company owner Kelly Anne, hopes her simple menu of classic comfort foods with a spritz of glitz will help her clean up. With a liberal sprinkling of confidence, a large dash of valleys warmth and some tried and tested comfort foods, she hopes to impress straight-talking bar manager Ryan, excitable local radio host Rachel, ultra-competitive tax consultant Runa and castle-owning energy consultant David

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Bar manager and former child actor Ryan hosts the second of this week's dinner parties, but before his guests have even sat down, a disaster in the kitchen has ruined the first course. Later, Ryan mixes up his steak orders, he has to stomach some harsh criticism

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Radio presenter Rachel hosts on the third day in Cardiff. Things get off to a bad start when she all but loses her voice. Rachel is undeterred however and expects her Indian menu to bag her the grand. Her guests are sceptical about Rachel's cooking abilities, and when accountant and self-proclaimed Asian cooking expert Runa realises what's on the menu, the gloves are off

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Accountant Runa is the fourth to host in Cardiff and is confident her Bangladeshi-inspired menu will wow her guests, having cooked this type of cuisine for more than 20 years. But she reckons without radio DJ Rachel, who is still smarting after Runa's criticism the previous night

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Energy consultant David hosts the final party in south Wales, putting on an evening of classic dinner-party fare, but his offal-based starter does not go down well with everyone. A revelation about his stately home leaves guests open-mouthed and one diner finds themselves banished to the dungeon

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay revisits three previous restaurants he tried to save, finding out whether they stayed on the road to recovery or turned back into nightmares. First he heads for West Babylon, New York, where he helped the inexperienced owners of restaurant Classic American deal with their escalating debts, before returning to Davide in Boston, an Italian restaurant run by two warring brothers. Finally, he visits Down City, a restaurant in Providence, Rhode Island, run by hot-headed owner Abby

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay revisits four restaurants he earlier tried to save, finding out whether they stayed on the road to recovery or fell by the wayside. First he visits the Spanish Pavillion in New Jersey, then heads to California to see the owners of Kingston Cafe, French-themed La Frite, and Capri

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: A look at what happened after Gordon Ramsay headed to Scottsdale, Arizona, where he met husband-and-wife bistro owners Samy and Amy Bouzaglo, who revealed a shocking contempt for customer feedback. The chef faced the biggest challenge of the series to save Amy's Baking Company - and a media furore unfolded after the episode was broadcast

programme.title: The Spiderwick Chronicles (2008)
programme.desc: Life for identical twins Jared and Simon and their sister Mallory begins afresh when their mother inherits a spooky mansion. But after moving in, strange things begin to happen, and as the youngsters investigate, they discover a book that opens up a mysterious world of amazing supernatural creatures. Fantasy adventure, starring Freddie Highmore in a dual role alongside Sarah Bolger and Nick Nolte

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Cut off from his father's fortune, Raj struggles to balance his books, so decides to appoint Sheldon to organise his finances and get him out of debt. Penny considers joining the guys at Comic-Con because she thinks Leonard wants her to come along

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Leonard and Penny offer Sheldon's old room to Raj after he is forced to move out of his apartment to save money, leading to unexpected conflict among the gang and some unwanted observations from Leonard's mother. Christine Baranski guests

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Leonard, Penny and Raj settle into their new living arrangement, but Penny and Raj get along so well together that Leonard starts to feel left out. Sheldon expresses an interest in Amy's work and they begin to collaborate on a scientific project

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon pushes himself to the limit when he collaborates on two scientific projects - one with Leonard and Howard, plus another with Amy - on the same day. Bernadette nears the end of her maternity leave, and looks forward to returning to work

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Howard and Bernadette find it upsetting to leave baby Halley at the nursery when Bernadette returns to work at the end of her maternity leave. The gang's university colleague Bert the geologist introduces them to his attractive new girlfriend Rebecca

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Leonard isn't thrilled when he finds out Penny is considering taking a new job that has been offered to her by her former boyfriend Zack. Sheldon attempts to develop his brain further by learning new skills

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Amy is offered a visiting research fellowship at Princeton University, and Raj makes some new living arrangements. When the guys complete their infinite persistence gyroscope project, events spin out of control

programme.title: Crocodile Dundee (1986)
programme.desc: A New York reporter searches the Australian Outback for a maverick hunter with a legendary reputation, and invites the adventurer back to America. But the die-hard Aussie needs to draw on all his survival skills to adjust to life in the urban jungle. Comedy adventure, written by and starring Paul Hogan, with Linda Kozlowski, John Meillon and Mark Blum

programme.title: The Inbetweeners Movie (2011)
programme.desc: Awkward teenagers Simon, Will, Jay and Neil plan a holiday to the Greek island of Crete after they finish their exams, hoping to experience drink, sex and debauchery beyond their wildest dreams. However, things do not quite work out as planned. Comedy based on the TV series, with Joe Thomas, Simon Bird, James Buckley and Blake Harrison

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: The households share their critiques of Britain's Got Talent, Peaky Blinders, The Great British Sewing Bee, Storm Troupers: The Fight to Forecast the Weather, Secret Life of the Human Pups, Big Fish Man, and the News. First shown in May 2016

programme.title: Batwoman
programme.desc: As Ryan focuses even more effort on taking down Black Mask, a new foe emerges diverting the team's attention to a distressing issue in Gotham. Continually haunted by the mistakes of his past, Jacob travels down a dangerous path to try to eradicate them, while Alice and Julia discover they have an enemy in common

programme.title: Naked Attraction
programme.desc: Dating show hosted by Anna Richardson in which two singletons choose a date from a selection of six naked people, who are revealed to them one body part at a time. This time, 30-year-old Pablo, who is HIV positive and hopes to break the stigma about his condition, and Leon, a musician and rapper from Burnley, take part

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: The gang become concerned when Sheldon's former admirer, Dr Ramona Nowitzki, resurfaces while Amy is away working at Princeton University. Jim Parsons stars

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Pilot episode of the comedy about scientists and their friendship with a down-to-earth neighbour. When aspiring actress Penny moves into Leonard's apartment block, he is immediately smitten, but Sheldon warns him that he runs the risk of a broken heart if he tries to woo her. Johnny Galecki, Jim Parsons and Kaley Cuoco star

programme.title: Black-ish
programme.desc: Dre's teenage cousin Kyra needs a place to stay and the Johnsons decide to take her in, while, Jack and Diane try to get information about her from social media

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: The fiery chef tries to change the fortunes of the Olde Stone Mill restaurant in Tuckahoe, New York, which is struggling to attract customers, leading to debts of nearly $1million. Gordon feels the owner is more interested in his own appearance than how his food tastes and a screaming match ensues

programme.title: Baby Daddy
programme.desc: Riley invites Ben to be her date at a work party, but his efforts to prove he is dependable fall short, and Danny hopes to come to her rescue instead. Meanwhile, Tucker schemes to go out with a model, and Bonnie attempts to get to know a handsome stranger. Starring Jean-Luc Bilodeau

programme.title: Baby Daddy
programme.desc: Ben discovers a shocking secret about Riley's new love Fitch, while things heat up between Danny and girlfriend Milena, and Tucker seizes an opportunity to impress his boss Chase. Comedy, starring Jean-Luc Bilodeau

programme.title: Melissa & Joey
programme.desc: Mel does her best to get the children excited about Halloween. Meanwhile, Lewis asks Joe for permission to spend one last evening with his kids before he goes to jail. Comedy, starring Melissa Joan Hart and Joey Lawrence

programme.title: Melissa & Joey
programme.desc: Joe decides to start an online business offering financial advice to investors and asks Mel for some tips on how to get the enterprise up and running. Meanwhile, Lennox develops feelings for a boy from school who is giving her extra academic tutoring. Family comedy, starring Melissa Joan Hart and Joey Lawrence

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Penny is forced to revise for a work assessment during the girls' trip to Las Vegas, and Amy and Bernadette accuse her of spoiling their fun. Meanwhile, the guys try to invent something of scientific importance - but only come up with new ways to procrastinate. Comedy, starring Johnny Galecki, Jim Parsons and Kaley Cuoco

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Leonard and Penny ask Howard and Bernadette for advice when they argue about money. Meanwhile, Sheldon and Raj try to find out whether they could survive a dark matter research expedition in a salt mine by attempting to simulate the conditions in a steam tunnel beneath the university. Comedy, starring Johnny Galecki, Jim Parsons and Kaley Cuoco

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Murray takes Adam to a Phillies baseball game and allows him to leave his seat on his own for the first time, only to find Adam immediately gets lost in the stadium. Beverly accompanies Erica and Barry on a shopping trip - but they begin arguing on the way home

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Erica decides to put college on hold and pursue her dream of becoming a pop singer - which causes her parents no end of stress. Meanwhile, an April Fools' Day prank leads to an all-out war between Adam and Barry. Comedy, starring Wendi McLendon-Covey, Jeff Garlin and Hayley Orrantia

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: The precinct's staff prepare for an unusually busy night as the neighbourhood gets into the Halloween spirit, and Amy falls into a particularly foul mood when she is forced to wear a fancy-dress costume during an undercover operation. Meanwhile, Jake bets Captain Holt that he can steal his Medal of Valour before midnight - a challenge that prompts him to don his own selection of disguises

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: Jake makes an arrest without much proof, and so has to spend the next 48 hours collecting enough evidence to keep his suspect in custody. While his co-workers are forced to help him out over the weekend, Charles judges a pie contest between Rosa and Gina, and the Captain gives advice to Terry, who has a problem involving his brother-in-law. Comedy, starring Andy Samberg

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Leonard and Penny ask Howard and Bernadette for advice when they argue about money. Meanwhile, Sheldon and Raj try to find out whether they could survive a dark matter research expedition in a salt mine by attempting to simulate the conditions in a steam tunnel beneath the university. Comedy, starring Johnny Galecki, Jim Parsons and Kaley Cuoco

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Beverly tries to help Adam navigate a painless path through puberty, but just as his voice is changing, he is cast in the lead role of the school musical. Determined to help him survive the performance, Beverly decides lip-synching might be the answer. Meanwhile, Murray is irked when Erica chooses musician Johnny over Barry's sidekick Geoff as her date for the homecoming dance

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Barry accidentally records over one of Beverly's prized family videotapes - her treasured keepsake from his fifth birthday party. Meanwhile, Adam and Murray build a robot together, but the project comes between them when they experience creative differences

programme.title: Black-ish
programme.desc: Dre's teenage cousin Kyra needs a place to stay and the Johnsons decide to take her in, while, Jack and Diane try to get information about her from social media

programme.title: Black-ish
programme.desc: Dre and Rainbow struggle with identity politics involving the kids, Diane's crush starts spending time with another girl, and Andre Jr's Valentine's Day date goes awry

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: Holt invites the entire squad to his house to celebrate his birthday, but Jeffords struggles to keep everyone in line, with Peralta and Santiago making a bad impression on their host's husband Kevin. Meanwhile, Boyle's love of food leads to a romantic connection with an older woman, and Diaz introduces Gina to some psychologists - who find her to be a fascinating subject

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: Boyle's new girlfriend gives his self-confidence a boost, but Peralta is forced to intervene before his colleague gets too serious, too soon in the relationship. Meanwhile, Diaz and Santiago have trouble taking a costumed crimefighter seriously, and Holt faces a challenge to his leadership

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon and Amy try to eliminate stress from the planning of their wedding by applying mathematics to the process. Meanwhile, Raj 'breaks up' with Howard after realising that his best friend is actually damaging his confidence

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon and Amy subject their friends to a series of secret tests to find out who is most qualified to act as best man and maid of honour at their wedding. Penny realises her true feelings about her friendship with Amy

programme.title: Hollyoaks
programme.desc: Drama following the lives and loves of a group of teenagers and young adults in Chester

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Interior designer James hosts the first of a week of dinner parties from Edinburgh, hoping to impress guests Aisling, Bruce and Katy with a spicy Mexican menu

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay heads for the Grasshopper Also in Carlstadt, New Jersey, a family-owned Irish restaurant. There, he lends a helping hand to Mitch Sandler, who has been struggling to live up to the expectations of his father-in-law since he invested heavily in the business. The chef also tries to improve the presentation of dishes emerging from the kitchen

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: Another chance to see the special Stand Up to Cancer edition of the TV review series, originally shown during the night of fundraising in October 2018, with Louis Tomlinson and Liam Payne, Danny and Dani Dyer, Dele Alli and Eric Dier, and Peter Crouch and Abbey Clancy taking to their sofas. What do they think of The Haunting of Hill House, The X Factor, University Challenge, The Yorkshire Vet, Naked Attraction and more?

programme.title: Rick and Morty
programme.desc: The Smiths suspect they are being hunted, but it remains to be seen who is real

programme.title: Harley Quinn
programme.desc: Harley and Ivy go undercover at college to take down the Riddler, who has made himself dean of the university and has sinister plans for the students - including Barbara Gordon

programme.title: Birdgirl
programme.desc: Judy partners with a tech therapy company and spills the company's deepest secrets. Featuring the voices of Paget Brewster and Rob Delaney

programme.title: Duncanville
programme.desc: Duncan's friends replace him in their online video game tournament, so he joins the opposition. Animated comedy about a 15-year-old boy with a rich fantasy life

programme.title: Naked Attraction
programme.desc: Dating show hosted by Anna Richardson in which two singletons choose a date from a selection of six naked people, who are revealed to them one body part at a time. This time, 28-year-old Michelle, a mother-of-two from Hampshire, and gym-loving Adam from Essex, who is 30, take part

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: The households share their critiques of Britain's Got Talent, Top Gear, Versailles, 70 with a Six Pack, Countryfile, 24 Hours in Police Custody, How to Get a Council House and the News from the comfort of their own homes. First shown in June 2016

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon helps Leonard drop off a parcel for Penny, but when he sees how messy her flat is, he tries to do a good deed - which is not well received. Johnny Galecki, Jim Parsons and Kaley Cuoco star

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Leonard is upset to learn Penny is dating someone, so his friends encourage him to seek a new romance by asking another woman out. Sara Gilbert appears alongside her Roseanne co-star Johnny Galecki

programme.title: Rick and Morty
programme.desc: The Smiths suspect they are being hunted, but it remains to be seen who is real

programme.title: Harley Quinn
programme.desc: Harley and Ivy go undercover at college to take down the Riddler, who has made himself dean of the university and has sinister plans for the students - including Barbara Gordon

programme.title: Baby Daddy
programme.desc: Ben offers to dog-sit for his girlfriend Megan, but the animal does not seem to like him much. Elsewhere, Tucker is angered by his room-mates' messy habits, so Danny hires someone to clean the apartment. Comedy, starring Jean-Luc Bilodeau

programme.title: Baby Daddy
programme.desc: Ben persuades Bonnie and Ray to invite his conservative Aunt Betty to Emma's baptism in the hope she will give the baby a generous gift, but this means hiding Ray's sexuality from her. Meanwhile, Riley makes Tucker and Danny compete to be chosen as the godfather

programme.title: Scrubs
programme.desc: With their wives out of town, JD and Turk plan to revisit their bachelor days by attending a party. Meanwhile, the students struggle with their final interviews with terminally ill patients, and Dr Kelso tries to find a way to say goodbye to Ted and Gooch as they prepare to embark on a journey to see the world. Medical comedy, starring Zach Braff and Donald Faison

programme.title: Scrubs
programme.desc: JD tries to win Turk's approval, while Lucy struggles to summon up the courage to perform a medical procedure. Elsewhere, Drew and Denise decide to make their relationship official

programme.title: Black-ish
programme.desc: Dre and Rainbow struggle with identity politics involving the kids, Diane's crush starts spending time with another girl, and Andre Jr's Valentine's Day date goes awry

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Kate Quilton heads to the Atlas Mountains in Morocco to uncover the secrets of saffron, revealing what makes it the most expensive spice in the world

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Helen Lawal heads to one of the world's biggest coffee factories to find out how instant cappuccinos get their froth. Matt Tebbutt is in Naples to discover why most tinned tomatoes come from Italy, while Kate Quilton embarks on a panettone odyssey across Europe to learn how the fluffy cake lasts so long

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Ben Hillman shows retired North Lanarkshire couple Bill and Maureen Hughes five properties in and around the town of Fort Myers, Florida, within their budget of £320,000. The pair hope to create a whole new chapter of family memories - and escape the ravages of the Scottish winter

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Chris and Anne-Marie McNeill from Scotland have fallen in love with the buzzy holiday atmosphere of south Tenerife after visiting regularly for seven years. Now they're looking for a second home where they can enjoy family holidays with their two children, so Laura Hamilton shows them five properties for their £200,000 budget

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Reg and Sarah Etwaroo from East Sussex are buying their first ever place together, and have their hearts set on a second home in the theme park capital of the world - Orlando in Florida. Ben Hillman shows the recently married couple properties for their £100,000 budget

programme.title: Bake Off: The Professionals
programme.desc: Tom Allen and Liam Charles present as the pastry chefs create miniature cone-shaped and carrot-shaped desserts - 24 of each. For the second challenge they must serve up a grand occasion showpiece - a Religieuse a l'Ancienne - to serve 24 people, with two different flavours of eclairs and inspired by the theme of time travel. Benoit Blin and Cherish Finden judge their efforts

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: A special programme celebrating the show's 20th anniversary, as Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer look back over two decades of house-hunting together. In this episode, the pair reminisce about some of their previous house-hunting missions where lifestyle needed to be balanced with business. Champion dog trainer Lauren needed land for her collies, while parents Carole and Alan wanted adjoining buildings to set up a holiday rental business.

programme.title: W Series Motor Racing
programme.desc: Coverage of the opening round of the season at Spielberg in Austria, as the second season of the all-female Formula 3-level racing series gets under way. Presented by Anna Woolhouse, with analysis from Naomi Schiff, David Coulthard and Billy Monger

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Another selection of B&B owners take it in turns as hosts to decide which of their establishments provides the best value for money. The contest begins at Ian and Sam Sugden's Abbey House in Penrith, Cumbria, where the guests are impressed with the cleanliness of the rooms. However, there is some grumbling over breakfast about the bacon

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The owners stay at Compton House in Newark, Nottinghamshire, which is run by Lisa Holloway - who prides herself on providing a home away from home for her visitors. However, some of the guests have issues with dust and the haddock served at breakfast - and challenge whether a night's stay is worth the price tag

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Dave and Jo Bate welcome their guests to the White Horse, a pub and B&B in Ripon, North Yorkshire, where they hope an afternoon of morris dancing in front of the cathedral will make a lasting impression. Unfortunately, Ed and Jane Mitchell get locked inside their room by mistake, and there are arguments over tomatoes at breakfast

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Retired teachers Ed and Jane Mitchell welcome the contestants to the Old Farmhouse, Cheshire, but everyone is shocked to discover they will have to share a bathroom. Meanwhile, Sam and Ian Sugden find an unwelcome surprise underneath their bed

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Payment day arrives, as the guests gather to find out who has won and to thrash out their differences - from some fishy findings to a reminder of why sorry can be the hardest word. Later on, a heated exchange about cleaning ends in tears

programme.title: Britain's Most Beautiful Landscapes
programme.desc: Exploring Northern Ireland's Causeway Coast between Londonderry and Belfast, taking in Mount Binevenagh, which has a lake perched on its summit, and the Giant's Causeway

programme.title: 999: On the Front Line
programme.desc: A woman at the airport suffers a suspected miscarriage, but the paramedics sent to her aid have trouble getting her back to hospital due to heavy snowfall. Crews also battle through the roads to treat a woman semi-conscious in police custody with collapsed veins

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: A teenager has a life-threatening bleed on the brain and requires emergency surgery, a 10-year-old gymnast sustains an injury to her wrist after performing a back bend, and a girl is brought in to the urgent care centre, needing treatment for her swollen tonsils. As they undergo treatment, their parents share stories of family life

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: A 75-year-old man who fell out of bed is suspected of having a stroke, and doctors have only a short window of time to administer a life-saving, clot-busting drug. A mother of two falls off her horse, and a six-year-old girl has a possible neck injury after an accident in the playground. Family members of the patients share their personal stories

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts the comedy words-and-numbers quiz, with captain Jon Richardson and Joe Wilkinson taking on guest captain Kevin Bridges and Jessica Knappett. John Cooper Clarke joins Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner, while Rachel Riley looks after the numbers and letters

programme.title: Father Ted
programme.desc: A sense of dread descends on Craggy Island as the priests prepare for poker-faced Father Stone's annual visit - and Ted tries desperately to think of a scheme to get rid of him. Clerical comedy, starring Dermot Morgan

programme.title: Father Ted
programme.desc: Bishop Brennan orders Ted and Dougal to campaign against a controversial film - but as a result of their protest it becomes the biggest box-office hit in Craggy Island's history. Comedy, starring Dermot Morgan, Ardal O'Hanlon, Frank Kelly and Pauline McLynn

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: A teenager has a life-threatening bleed on the brain and requires emergency surgery, a 10-year-old gymnast sustains an injury to her wrist after performing a back bend, and a girl is brought in to the urgent care centre, needing treatment for her swollen tonsils. As they undergo treatment, their parents share stories of family life

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: A 75-year-old man who fell out of bed is suspected of having a stroke, and doctors have only a short window of time to administer a life-saving, clot-busting drug. A mother of two falls off her horse, and a six-year-old girl has a possible neck injury after an accident in the playground. Family members of the patients share their personal stories

programme.title: Father Ted
programme.desc: A sense of dread descends on Craggy Island as the priests prepare for poker-faced Father Stone's annual visit - and Ted tries desperately to think of a scheme to get rid of him. Clerical comedy, starring Dermot Morgan

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Jimmy Doherty is in Thailand asking how the blossom from a banana tree is shaking up fish and chip suppers. Matt Tebbutt visits Spain to find out how the delicate raspberry gets from fields to fridges without turning to mush, and Andi Oliver heads to an onion factory to see how the eye-watering vegetables are peeled on a gigantic scale

programme.title: George Clarke's Amazing Spaces
programme.desc: The architect meets a teacher planning to turn a double-decker bus into a mobile classroom, while a family of car enthusiasts from Coventry is working on a camping trailer. Plus, the Spanish tour continues with a visit to a 100-year-old farmhouse on the coast of Asturias, and the Big Build team design a lightweight, flexible and multi-functional wall system for the campervan living pod

programme.title: Ugly House to Lovely House with George Clarke
programme.desc: Award-winning architect Jo Cowen, who has worked on projects including the British Museum and the JP Morgan tower at Canary Wharf, is called in to help Croydon couple Denise and Ali breathe new life into their uninspiring council house. She plans a striking glazed extension, and comes up with a further idea for the upstairs that requires an extra £40,000 on top of the not inconsiderable £120,000 budget. But problems begin to appear, pushing the couple to the brink

programme.title: George Clarke's Old House, New Home
programme.desc: The host helps Leicester couple James and Drew restore their period house, reclaiming its classic Georgian dimensions and features. In Brixton, south London, a Victorian living room has had all its period features ripped out and replaced with 1970s kitsch and fake Tudor. Can George turn this travesty into a smart and sophisticated family sitting room? Plus, he visits a 300-year-old silk weaver's house in Spitalfields, London, that is perfectly adapted for stylish modern living

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Another selection of B&B owners take it in turns as hosts to decide which of their establishments provides the best value for money. The contest begins at Knockaloe Beg Farm on the Isle of Man, where owners Fiona and John Anderson hope their guests will embrace their green ethos. They kick things off with an afternoon of laser mayhem, and continue the fun the next day by following breakfast with hands-on animal antics

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The guests head to the Arches on the Isle of Man for their second stay, where owner Phil Routledge hopes to wow them with scenic views and scuba diving at the B&B's own pool. However, some of the owners are dismayed by modern gadgets with a lack of instructions, and even find cobwebs in their rooms

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants arrive at the Trafford in Blackpool, owned by Robbie and John Welling, who are proud of their traditional 'bucket and spade' B&B. The guests are pleasantly surprised, even if previous host Phil is intent on finding dust as a bit of tit-for-tat retaliation, and the afternoon is spent learning the art of making sticks of rock

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Host Julie welcomes the contestants to Sheepy Lodge, Warwickshire, where they are shocked by their surroundings, and disappointed by a variety of things in the rooms. The host puts on some old-fashioned parlour games, before a fierce hide-and-seek contest erupts. At breakfast, Julie frets when she must prepare poached eggs, and is left emotional after the feedback

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The rival B&B owners meet to settle some scores and discover what they have been paid. Knockaloe Beg Farm owners Fiona and John Anderson tackle criticisms of a missing host at breakfast, and Phil Routledge from The Arches on the Isle of Man takes on Robbie and John Welling from The Trafford in Blackpool about their 'forensic investigation'. After some heated discussion, the winner is announced

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Dick and Angel as they host a very special wedding for close friends. Motorcycle-mad Essex couple Martin and Kim start their search to buy the perfect chateau but can't see eye to eye. Cash-strapped Tim and Rebecca call in their mates to help with restoration work. Fiona puts her B&B plans on hold as she races against time to prepare for her first wedding. With the reception set to be outside she needs to fix 40 windows on the ageing chateau facade

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Tim and Rebecca battle to get their chateau looking tip top for a tour operator who may potentially want to put it on his books for visitors to the nearby Le Mans motor racing track. Motorcycle-loving couple Martin and Kim continue their roller coaster search for a dream chateau. The clock is ticking for Fiona as she has 24 hours to prepare for her first ever wedding, and ensure that her 12-acre grounds are picture perfect for the big day. Karen, Paul and their two children need to make their chateau attractive to guests. Their latest plan is to restore a disused hen house, but tensions rise when Paul doesn't meet Karen's standards

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Another selection of B&B owners take it in turns as hosts to decide which of their establishments provides the best value for money. The contest begins at Dunsandles Guest House in New Brighton, Merseyside, where owner Alan Johnson and his professional sidekick Dave Owen hope to win the title - but a scandal with some unwanted hair on the bed sheets is not a good start, and two guests feel they are making an exhibition of themselves with a tea-trolley in full view of the neighbours

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: On the second day of the competition, the contestants visit the Lady Teal Hotel Boat on the waterways near Burnley in Lancashire, and owners Nick and Gina Mead hope to impress them with an afternoon of entertainment out on the locks. At breakfast, one of the guests admits to not enjoying his stay

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: From York House in Tetbury, Gloucestershire, where Brock Bergius hopes that his cheeky sense of humour and perfectionist nature will help him win the competition. He is in his element making saucy jokes during an afternoon of sausage making, but his reluctance to stray from the menu at breakfast leaves some of his guests disappointed

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition heads to Heyford House in Oxfordshire, where Leo and Sonya Brooke-Little hope to impress the contestants with their hospitality. The afternoon event is boxing, which Brock Burgius sees as the perfect opportunity to settle some scores with his fellow competitors

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The week's winner is announced as the B&B owners meet for the last time to thrash out their differences. Feedback includes criticism of a badly positioned tea tray, controversy over a hot chocolate and accusations of overenthusiastic hosting

programme.title: 999: On the Front Line
programme.desc: Paramedics Jan Vann and Chelsie Kennedy rush to a road traffic collision, but find the driver of the car has left the vehicle and doesn't want to be treated by the crew. The police suspect he may have been drinking, but the crew need to convince him to receive their help. In Coventry, paramedics Neil and Laura rush to a two-year-old boy who is having a severe allergic reaction

programme.title: 999: What's Your Emergency?
programme.desc: An edition focusing on the behaviour of teenage boys, with frontline staff in Northamptonshire and across the country saying they are becoming more violent. PC Kathleen Roberts responds to a suspected acid attack carried out by a teenager in Northampton, while Sgt Martin Waller is dispatched to the scene of a suspected gang-related stabbing

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Mark, 32, is brought to resus with a head injury after having an epileptic seizure, and he is sent for an urgent CT scan to assess the damage to his brain. Anastasia, 80, comes to urgent care with a pain in her leg and while she is sent for an X-ray to rule out a blood clot, she shares the story of her arrival to study in England from Nigeria, only for civil war to break out back home. Porter Chris, who's been part of the A&E department for 25 years, shares his insights into the job as he goes about his day

programme.title: Emergency Helicopter Medics
programme.desc: Helen is trapped in her car after a crash on the A66, while golfer Michael has collapsed with a suspected cardiac arrest in Hertfordshire. Plus, 15-year-old Jamie is treated for a broken jaw and possible spinal injury after falling 10 feet

programme.title: 999: On the Front Line
programme.desc: Paramedics Jan Vann and Chelsie Kennedy rush to a road traffic collision, but find the driver of the car has left the vehicle and doesn't want to be treated by the crew. programme.title: Kirstie's Vintage Gems
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp joins jewellery-maker Rachel Jeffrey to help a soon-to-be-married couple create their own wedding rings, and learns how gold can revive an old picture frame at little cost

programme.title: Heir Hunters
programme.desc: The probate researchers are surprised when a new heir comes to light on a case they thought was complete, and a story of wartime aviation is revealed. Plus, a woman tries to find out more about her deceased cousin who was a chess champion and playwright

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Max McMurdo head to Wales to see how can create the most profit on items, including an oil dispenser, fire grate, chaise longue and even a catamaran

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien attempt to restore items including an old cheese press, and a 1980s motorbike almost gets the better of Henry and his restorer Guy

programme.title: Car S.O.S.
programme.desc: Tim, Fuzz and the team work on a 1963 Alfa Romeo Giulia Spider that has been kept in a dusty barn in Essex after its owner's plans to restore it were interrupted by the onset of a serious medical condition

programme.title: A New Life in the Sun
programme.desc: A Scottish couple move to a millionaire's playground, two restaurateurs branch out into weddings and a pair of adrenaline junkies diversify into the B&B business

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition heads to Anglesey, where best pals Richard Tuke and Susan Hatch hope to show that Blackthorn Farm B&B, which they own, is better than any of their guests' establishments. However, they find that their close friendship is scrutinised even more than their property

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The White Swan Hotel in Yeadon, West Yorkshire, is the second stop of the competition, and owners Graham and Barbara Yellow hope their basic facilities and affordable prices will please their guests. Spiritual couple Malcolm and Fiona Piers-Taylor get good vibes from their room, Rachel Lonergan views the hosts in a new light and breakfast is well received by the contestants, despite having to pay a supplement fee

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Vegetarians Malcolm and Fiona Piers-Taylor host at the Old Mill House B&B in Bridport, Dorset, and both come under attack at dinner when Malcolm voices his opinions on meat eating. In the morning, a meditation session gets a mixed response, and most of the guests are left cold by the vegetarian buffet breakfast

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Rachel and Dan Lonergan welcome their guests to the Brighton Inn, East Sussex, where they hope to offer a luxury, boutique experience. Malcolm and Fiona Piers-Taylor give their room a thorough inspection, before the groups learns the art of building a human pyramid in the afternoon's activity. At breakfast, they discover a lack of vegetarian alternatives, and the hosts suspect game-playing when they are shocked by some negative feedback

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&B owners come together to confront their rivals and find out how much they have been paid. Richard and Susan are keen to tackle the bathroom issues of Barbara and Graham, who bask in the comments from Malcolm and Fiona. The Old Mill House proprietors defend their vegetarian breakfast routine, but serve up snobby feedback to Rachel and Dan from the Brighton Inn

programme.title: Grand Designs New Zealand
programme.desc: Musicians Justine Cormack and Marc Taddei swap their Auckland home for the mountains of Central Otago. They risk all by adapting an American design for the Southern Hemisphere

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: A dachshund is brought in by owners Kelly and Daniel to see if anything can be done to repair a deformity in its foot, and Noel Fitzpatrick gives the one-year-old a lesson in doggy discipline along the way. A 10-month-old labrador needs a hip replacement before it can begin training as a medical-alert dog to help its owner with her rare health condition. An eight-year-old shih-tzu has the most extraordinary walk the vet has ever seen, but the operation to fix the dog's spine will be extremely risky, presenting the owners with an agonising decision

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau
programme.desc: Fresh from the success of hosting a three-day wedding at the chateau, Dick and Angel make business plans for the grounds, intending to charge people to fish in the moat and forage in the woods. Angel starts work on a second guest suite, but also wants to install an elevator, which pushes Dick's engineering skills to the limit

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud follows software executive Andy and garden designer Nicki, who are planning to build a 'floating' house on the Thames, which would be the first of its kind in the UK. Their architect has come up with an ambitious, experimental design that would see the whole property magically rise up with the flood waters. However, no one knows whether it will work, and the project soon becomes bogged down in the logistical challenges of bringing to fruition such a complex structure on a small island that is only accessible by a narrow footbridge

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud follows more of Britain's most ambitious self-building projects, as intrepid individuals attempt to design and construct the home of their dreams. In this first edition, Kevin meets former army captain Justin, who has spent £1.8million on an abandoned neo-Gothic cemetery keeper's lodge and adjoining ex-council toilet block in south-west London. Justin's vision is to restore the lodge but demolish the ugly toilet blocks and replace them with an enormous luxury ground-floor extension, with an added moat and a giant basement with a swimming pool - all just metres from the nearest grave

programme.title: George Clarke's Amazing Spaces
programme.desc: The architect meets a West Sussex couple planning to build a house on the back of an old trailer, while in Wiltshire, a single mother plans to transform an old military helicopter, and a water tower undergoes a surprising conversion in Warwickshire. George's tour of Spain continues in northern Andalusia, where he visits a cave-style retreat quarried deep into the mountains

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: The return of the documentary about life inside an A&E department, following patients treated in the same 24-hour period at St George's in south-west London. Fourteen-year-old Eduard is rushed in after falling from a swing and fracturing his ankle, while 62-year-old Rosina is treated having broken her wrist on the dancefloor at a family party. Paula is in Resus having been unable to take in food through her feeding tube and it is not only her health that is in danger, but also that of her unborn baby

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts the comedy panel show's version of the words-and-numbers quiz, with Lee Mack and Victoria Coren Mitchell taking on Alan Carr and Dane Baptiste. Sam Simmons joins Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner, while Rachel Riley looks after the numbers and letters

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud follows more of Britain's most ambitious self-building projects, as intrepid individuals attempt to design and construct the home of their dreams. In this first edition, Kevin meets former army captain Justin, who has spent £1.8million on an abandoned neo-Gothic cemetery keeper's lodge and adjoining ex-council toilet block in south-west London. Justin's vision is to restore the lodge but demolish the ugly toilet blocks and replace them with an enormous luxury ground-floor extension, with an added moat and a giant basement with a swimming pool - all just metres from the nearest grave

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: The return of the documentary about life inside an A&E department, following patients treated in the same 24-hour period at St George's in south-west London. Fourteen-year-old Eduard is rushed in after falling from a swing and fracturing his ankle, while 62-year-old Rosina is treated having broken her wrist on the dancefloor at a family party. Paula is in Resus having been unable to take in food through her feeding tube and it is not only her health that is in danger, but also that of her unborn baby

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Jimmy Doherty visits the largest wholesale food market in the world in Paris and discovers why British cheese sales are soaring across Europe. Matt Tebbutt travels to one of the largest ham producers in Italy and meets a pig farmer who tells him about a delicate problem that all pork producers face, while Kate Quilton investigates why some limes are so much juicier than others

programme.title: Private Lives
programme.desc: Dr Tracy Borman, Chief Curator at Historic Royal Palaces, explores Edward VIII's childhood with his overbearing father, reveals how the First World War saw him grow up in more ways than one, and explores his apparent desire for married women. She also uncovers the truth about Edward's abdication of the throne, and Wallis Simpson's infidelity, and reveals how Edward was a fashion icon of the day, examining his clothes in Kensington Palace

programme.title: War Factories
programme.desc: How the Americans, once they were provoked into war, out-produced the rest of the world so quickly and by such a huge margin, including the likes of General Motors

programme.title: War Factories
programme.desc: Henry Ford's plan, with the help of Albert Kahn, to build a giant factory in Michigan, covering 3.5million sq ft and including an aircraft production line half a mile in length

programme.title: War Factories
programme.desc: The sabotaging efforts of Peugeot in Nazi-occupied France, where the bosses and workers cooperated closely with the Resistance to hinder the use of military vehicles by the Nazis

programme.desc: Dave heads to Bolton to pick up a classic Italian sports car - a Lancia Fulvia 1.3 Sport styled by Milan coachbuilder Zagato

programme.title: Restoration Workshop
programme.desc: Antiques dealer and expert restorer Gary Wallis and his team of highly skilled craftspeople turn the tired and unloved items into objects that will turn a large profit. In the opening episode, they restore a Grenadier guard's drum, create a garden feature from a helicopter tripod and re-purpose a vintage car grille into a gleaming design classic

programme.title: Restoration Workshop
programme.desc: Gary Wallis re-invents an old ships compass and a grand display cabinet into two of his most successful transformations to date

programme.title: Restoration Workshop
programme.desc: Gary Wallis visits an automobilia auction at Silverstone, creates a chair out of Second World War bomber jackets and gives a Victorian table a new zinc top

programme.title: Restoration Workshop
programme.desc: Gary Wallis visits Kempton Park Autojumble. He buys a tired vintage Moto Guzzi bike and transforms it into stunning display piece

programme.title: Wonders of the Universe
programme.desc: Professor Brian Cox explores how the development of mankind and the history of the universe are intertwined, visiting dramatic locations around the world to illustrate the fundamental scientific principles that govern the laws of nature. He begins by investigating time, charting the evolution of the cosmos and explaining why the universe must one day come to an end

programme.title: Wonders of the Universe
programme.desc: Professor Brian Cox continues his exploration of the origins of mankind by investigating the matter from which everything on Earth is formed. At a Hindu cremation in Nepal, he discusses the faith's philosophy of life as an eternal cycle of creation and destruction, and draws a parallel with the lives of the universe's stars - before revealing why everything on Earth was once a part of something else

programme.title: Wonders of the Universe
programme.desc: Professor Brian Cox explores how gravity influences events across the universe, discovering why such a relatively weak force dictates Earth's orbit around the Sun - as well as the solar system's movement through the galaxy. He also explains why the gravity of a neutron star is much stronger than that of Earth, and looks back through history to reveal why scientists' research into the power of attraction has given mankind a far greater understanding of the cosmos

programme.title: Wonders of the Universe
programme.desc: Professor Brian Cox reveals how the unique properties of light provide an insight into the origins and evolution of both mankind and the universe. He charts the importance of light in the development of the world's ancient civilisations, and explains how the speed of light allows the measurement of both distance and time - before pinpointing one of the most important moments in the evolution of life on Earth

programme.title: One Foot in the Grave
programme.desc: The Meldrews, Mrs Warboys and Nick Swainey spend a day boating on the river, but get marooned - so Victor sets out alone to fetch help and stumbles across a disturbing secret at an old people's home. Richard Wilson stars

programme.title: One Foot in the Grave
programme.desc: The Meldrews return home from a dreary holiday to find the neighbours about to sell their house to a weirdo - and while Victor is busy moaning about everything under the sun, Margaret considers embarking on an affair. Comedy, starring Richard Wilson and Annette Crosbie

programme.title: One Foot in the Grave
programme.desc: Victor is stuck at home while waiting to be called for jury service - but as time ticks on and his frustration grows, he begins to feel as if he is the one on trial. Richard Wilson stars in an episode featuring no other characters

programme.title: Guitar Heroes at the BBC
programme.desc: Archive footage from BBC music shows of the 1970s, including Pete Townshend of the Who, acoustic numbers by Davy Graham and Ralph McTell, and the talents of Americans Nils Lofgren and Ted Nugent. Also featured are performances by the Faces, Chris Spedding, the Runaways, Judas Priest, ZZ Top and the Cate Brothers

programme.title: Fleetwood Mac - Don't Stop
programme.desc: Documentary looking back over the band's long career, from the early blues outfit led by Peter Green in the late 1960s, to their reincarnation in the 1970s with the line-up that went on to record the 40-million-selling album Rumours. Mick Fleetwood, John McVie, Lindsey Buckingham and Stevie Nicks reveal the truth behind the successes, failures and relationships that characterised their music and made them one of the most enduring acts in popular music

programme.title: Private Lives
programme.desc: Dr Tracy Borman, Chief Curator at Historic Royal Palaces, travels to Paris to reveal Napoleon's relationships, from nervous first encounters with a prostitute to his all-consuming love for Josephine, his Empress consort. She visits the statesman and military leader's former home Malmaison and the Palace of Fontainebleau to view his throne room and reveals the secrets to his extraordinary success - and calamitous downfall

programme.title: Private Lives
programme.desc: Chief Curator at Historic Royal Palaces, Dr Tracy Borman travels to Germany to uncover Nazi Party leader Adolf Hitler's abusive childhood and artistic aspirations. She reveals how he was shaped by the First World War and uncovers his relationships with much younger women, including his own niece, discovering how the personal motivations that came from his private life unleashed so much destruction

programme.title: The Great War in Numbers
programme.desc: Documentary exploring the First World War through numbers, beginning with a look at the growth of empires in the years leading up to 1914 and the fatal first shot

programme.title: The Great War in Numbers
programme.desc: The development of new weapons technology, with a look at how machine guns completely changed the nature of warfare and the increased use of planes and U-boats

programme.title: The Great War in Numbers
programme.desc: The key events of 1916, examining the unprecedented levels of recruitment by both sides, and the thousands of deaths in the battles of Verdun and the Jutland Peninsula

programme.title: WW2 - Battles for Europe
programme.desc: Eye-witness veteran testimony from men who served under fire on the frontline, taking viewers to the heart of iconic moments in the history of warfare. The first episode looks at the Allied invasion of Europe, which began on June 6 1944 - D-Day. British, American and Canadian troops stormed the beaches of Normandy in what would become known as The Longest Day

programme.title: WW2 - Battles for Europe
programme.desc: This edition examines the efforts of the British and Canadians who fought elite German divisions to capture the city of Caen, the key to breaking out of Normandy

programme.title: WW2 - Battles for Europe
programme.desc: Examining the work of the American forces who launched a huge offensive to blast a gap in the German lines and break through to liberate France

programme.title: WW2 - Battles for Europe
programme.desc: Field Marshal Montgomery's daring airborne plan to end the war by Christmas, which went a bridge too far at Arnhem

programme.title: Secrets of the Transport Museum
programme.desc: The legendary Napier-Railton has a service and some treasure is found in the museum tower, while a 100-year-old push bike gets a wooden wheel built

programme.title: Royals on the Frontline

programme.title: Royals on the Frontline

programme.title: Bangers and Cash

programme.title: Private Lives
programme.desc: Dr Tracy Borman, Chief Curator at Historic Royal Palaces, travels to Paris to reveal Napoleon's relationships, from nervous first encounters with a prostitute to his all-consuming love for Josephine, his Empress consort. She visits the statesman and military leader's former home Malmaison and the Palace of Fontainebleau to view his throne room and reveals the secrets to his extraordinary success - and calamitous downfall

programme.title: Private Lives
programme.desc: Chief Curator at Historic Royal Palaces, Dr Tracy Borman travels to Germany to uncover Nazi Party leader Adolf Hitler's abusive childhood and artistic aspirations. She reveals how he was shaped by the First World War and uncovers his relationships with much younger women, including his own niece, discovering how the personal motivations that came from his private life unleashed so much destruction

programme.title: Private Lives

programme.title: Private Lives

programme.title: The Life of Mammals

programme.desc: Jane and Jilly from Brighton look for a holiday home in the Dordogne region of south-west France. Laura Hamilton shows them five suitable properties for their £100,000 budget

programme.title: Coast vs Country
programme.desc: Kerr Drummond shows Tom and Colette the very best of what their £1,200,000 budget can get them on the Dorset coast, while Kirsty Duffy takes them to view rural properties

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Karen and Julian run a B&B in Cornwall and although they've never been to Naxos, they've decided it's the perfect place for a holiday home. Scarlette Douglas sets out to help them find the perfect pad on a budget of £215,000

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Jasmine Harman helps George Robertson from Bedfordshire search for a second home in Majorca's Port de Pollensa, a place he has visited over 30 times

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer look back at 20 years of trying to find homes for people with very specific criteria and must-haves. Ghika and Ronnie's search area is roughly two square miles, and they have been looking for the perfect house for a decade. Surfing is Jamie's passion, so he and wife Sam are looking for somewhere a stone's throw from the beach. Musicians Me'sha and Darren have viewed 70 properties but cannot seem to let go of anything on their wish list

programme.title: George Clarke's Amazing Spaces
programme.desc: George travels to the Cornish village of Charlestown, where Charlotte and Joey have bought a public loo that they are planning to turn into a luxury clifftop holiday home - at a cost of £65,000. He also meets a Bristol family who are making their own safari tent, completes his tour of New Zealand with a visit to an underground house, and he and Will Hardie finally unveil their prototype futuristic home

programme.title: In the Footsteps of Killers
programme.desc: Emilia Fox and David Wilson investigate the 1967 murder of 19-year-old RAF servicewoman Rita Ellis, whose body was found by a dog walker near her base, having been killed in a brutal attack. Despite a number of leads the police have never managed to catch Rita's murderer and with a case that's 53 years old, Emilia and David have a challenge tracking down witnesses, other potential victims and a suspect

programme.title: 999: What's Your Emergency?
programme.desc: The realities of policing home drinking, and what happens when parties go very badly wrong, seen through the eyes of South Yorkshire Police's frontline officers. PCs Alice Marshall and Matt Spaven investigate a house party that's a concern for neighbours, while PCs Mohammed Alrobaidi and Bernice Gott find themselves at risk at a rowdy house party in a quiet street. In Rotherham, PCs Pete Ellard and Tabitha Rumney have their hands full with a drunk and disorderly couple who are violently resisting arrest

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: A nurse shares her story of finding a career she loves, and doctors suspect a 93-year-old man with a low heart rate, dizziness and confusion may need a new pacemaker fitting. A construction worker whose finger has been crushed discusses the importance of male role models in his life, and a haemophiliac reflects on living with his condition

programme.title: Celebrity Gogglebox
programme.desc: Nick and Liv Grimshaw, Denise van Outen and Eddie Boxshall, Mo Gilligan and Babatunde Aleshe, Shaun Ryder and Bez, Martin and Roman Kemp, Gyles Brandreth and Maureen Lipman, Lorraine Kelly and Rosie Smith, Chris Eubank Sr and Chris Eubank Jr, and Eamonn Holmes and Ruth Langsford assess the week's week's biggest news stories, hard-hitting documentaries, laughable comedies and gritty dramas

programme.title: Dogging Tales
programme.desc: True Stories documentary by photographer Leo Maguire, providing an insight into why men and women engage in or watch sexual activity in front of strangers in public areas after dark. Accompanying his participants to lay-bys, woods and picnic spots around the UK, they discuss their attraction to dogging and reveal how they were introduced to it

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Bart learns that he was once a child celebrity, but that Homer spent all the money he made, so he separates from his parents and moves into a loft apartment, where his neighbours are punk band Blink-182 and skateboarding hero Tony Hawk

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Homer develops a ravenous appetite for Krustyburger's new spare rib sandwich, while Lisa is offered a scholarship at the college of her choice - but only if she agrees to deliberately lose a spelling contest

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Glamorous Hollywood star Sara Sloane visits Springfield and becomes infatuated with Ned Flanders, who finds himself asking Homer for dating advice. Featuring the voices of Helen Fielding and Marisa Tomei

programme.title: Posh Pawnbrokers
programme.desc: In Sheffield, London Road Pawnbrokers boss Dan takes a test flight in a plane that's for sale and looks at a gold collection with some hidden gems and a stunning diamond solitaire. Meanwhile, in south-east London, boss of Pickwick Pawnbrokers Nathan is considering doing deals on an oriental statue, a luxury handbag and a pricey timepiece

programme.title: Posh Pawnbrokers
programme.desc: London Road boss Dan Hatfield considers deals on luxury luggage and a sapphire ring. Meanwhile, brothers Patrick and Charlie Devine are keen to strike a deal over a pair of saunas, and a designer watch arouses their interest

programme.title: Posh Pawnbrokers
programme.desc: In London Road, Dan and assistant Ellie are busy doing deals on an antique diamond ring, Whitney Houston's autograph and a £45,000 collection of jewellery. At Nikolas Patrick in London, Charlie and Patrick look at a classic electric guitar and an expensive watch that was a gift from the Sultan of Oman

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Emma and Paul take on a massive garden project as they clear a lake bed with the aim of connecting it to their current lake, while they also bring their vine house back to life by creating an al fresco dining area. Mariam and Johnno turn on the style as they launch their chateau as an events venue by inviting locals over for a 1930s-themed party. And Karen and Paul's hopes for the stable and coach house to become a wedding venue are put in jeopardy as the costs of the roof renovations spiral out of control

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Mariam and Johnno restore their chateau's original bread oven for a sourdough weekend. Angel goes bargain hunting to find a unique radiator cover for the chateau's boot room, while Emma and Paul complete the final room in the west wing and theme it after the renowned artist Alphonse Mucha.

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Danni Menzies helps Colin and Elspeth from Edinburgh search for a holiday home in Menorca, which they can enjoy well into their retirement with their family

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Danni Menzies finds five potential properties for Jill Castling, who has dreamt of owning a family holiday home in Barbados for as long as she can remember

programme.title: Ramsay's 24 Hours to Hell and Back
programme.desc: Gordon travels to the heart of Los Angeles, California, to save the Brownstone Bistro, a Caribbean fusion restaurant. The chef and his team have just 24 hours to renovate the premises with a vibrant look, create more space in the cramped kitchen and introduce a simplified menu featuring fresh and authentic Caribbean fare, working around the clock to teach the staff how to cook the dishes

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Fiery chef Gordon Ramsay comes to the aid of a mother-and-son team who have gone through 20 chefs in two years at their struggling Italian restaurant in California. The adjoining bar is a triumph, but the oldest dining establishment in town needs an overhaul if the inexperienced owners are to make a profit

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Jo and Darrell from south-west London are looking for a holiday home on the Balearic Island of Menorca to enjoy with their two young children and Darrell's grown-up son. They run their own busy human resources company together so cannot wait to find a bolthole to relax in and have some quality time as a family. With a budget of £300,000 Danni Menzies is on hand to show them five properties around the south of the island

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: John, a town planner, and Daniel a luxury retail manager, have been together for five years and feel now is the time to find themselves a dream holiday home for their budget of £100,000. Keen to get their teeth into a renovation project, they've chosen the area around Cádiz in southern Spain. Ben Hillman lines up five property options to consider

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: Make Do and Mend
programme.desc: Dick and Angel Strawbridge help Kate and Erik restore their 150-year-old sash window and turn their small landing into a beautiful space for all the family that not only looks amazing and brings the past back to life, but saves thousands of pounds. Angel reveals the art of creating repeat patterns on fabric as a unique solution to Jo and family's desire to display their precious vintage family photos in something other than a frame. Dick solves Suzanna and Luke's conundrum of how to turn a treasured wardrobe into a showcase for shoes, by designing ingenious shelving inspired by a visit to the kitchen

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Noel treats chocolate Labrador Inca, who has a history of developmental elbow disease and osteoarthritis that has led to her elbow joints becoming worn out. Two-year-old Cavapoo Nala, who has hip dysplasia, and Great Dane Charles, who is unsteady on his feet due to a squashed spinal cord, are also on the patients list. Charles' owner Steve knows only too well that spinal surgery is a hugely serious undertaking, as he himself had multiple surgeries following a bike accident a decade earlier

programme.title: Ramsay's 24 Hours to Hell and Back
programme.desc: Extended edition in which Gordon Ramsay revitalises three businesses in Ellicott City, Maryland, which suffered flood damage in 2016 and 2018. It's such a huge undertaking that for the first time ever, Gordon dumps the 24-hour time limit

programme.title: Naked Attraction
programme.desc: A final romp through the Naked Attraction archive, remembering the times that the pickers got it spot on and chose their perfect date, leading to romance and happy endings. The success stories include Gemma and James, who found love on the show and whose relationship is still going strong

programme.title: Dogging Tales
programme.desc: True Stories documentary by photographer Leo Maguire, providing an insight into why men and women engage in or watch sexual activity in front of strangers in public areas after dark. programme.title: In the Footsteps of Killers

Despite a number of leads the police have never managed to catch Rita's murderer and with a case that's 53 years old, Emilia and David have a challenge tracking down witnesses, other potential victims and a suspect

programme.title: Great Canal Journeys
programme.desc: Sheila Hancock and broadcaster Gyles Brandreth tackle their first manual lock and some terrifyingly tight turns, but find themselves relaxing into life at 4mph, and marvel at how secret histories reveal themselves. They visit the Royal Gunpowder Mills, which built its own canal system to transport volatile ammunition - vital to Britain's success in both world wars

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Moe desperately wants to raise cash to lavish on his new girlfriend, so Homer rashly agrees to help him defraud his car insurance company - but his bumbling attempts to destroy the vehicle put him behind bars. Featuring the guest voice of Helen Hunt

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition begins at the Edge Accommodation in Huddersfield, West Yorkshire, where owner John Whitworth is hoping to win over his guests with his unique style and personality

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Young-at-heart romantics Willie and Julie kick off this competition in the Cambridge area with their hearty but simple menu featuring a boar main course. The subsequent entertainers are sporty pair Jeavon and Steve, and Italian Rica and Lynne

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Karen and Julian run a B&B in Cornwall and although they've never been to Naxos, they've decided it's the perfect place for a holiday home. Scarlette Douglas sets out to help them find the perfect pad on a budget of £215,000

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Jasmine Harman helps potential buyers Ali Harris and Steve Ashby from Dorset to explore the region of Abruzzo in southern Italy, where they dream of retiring to run a B&B

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: With their first baby on the way, Shoba and Rayhan Aziz have decided a holiday home close to glitzy Marbella in Spain would be the perfect place for them and their growing family to unwind. Jonnie Irwin guides them through the local market, showing them five properties for their £170,000 budget

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Gemma Willock hosts the first party in the West Midlands, and her evening of glitz and glamour looks set to be a roaring success until an unwelcome revelation about her corner-cutting approach to the competition threatens her chances of taking the £1,000 prize

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Mortgage adviser Andrew Devey throws the second party in the West Midlands, hosting a beatnik-themed evening. He cooks shepherd's pie for his main course, as well as lining up some pop art in the hope that it will expand his guests' minds

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Vegetarian Jules Evans hosts the third day of the competition in the West Midlands, and plans to indulge her passion for historical re-enactments by planning a medieval-themed dinner. The guests offer mixed responses when they look at the menu, but things take a turn for the better when she announces a duel will form part of the evening's entertainment

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Massage therapist Lucy Hird hosts the fourth dinner party from the West Midlands, featuring an extensive menu full of Thai specialities. She also gives individual presents to her guests, but her night is compromised by the entertainment

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Mental-health nurse Lloyd Cast hosts the final dinner party from the West Midlands. Despite appearing to be an extrovert so far, he decides on a chilled theme for the evening to allow his food to take centre stage, before the winner of the £1,000 is announced

programme.title: Ramsay's 24 Hours to Hell and Back
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay is in Los Angeles, where he tries to bring family-owned Mexican restaurant La Serenata back from the brink of disaster. But what will the customers think of the changes?

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Emma and Paul prepare for their biggest chateau booking ever as they host American veterans for the 75th anniversary of D-Day. programme.title: Dogs Behaving (Very) Badly
programme.desc: Trainer Graeme Hall tries to solve dog behavioural problems, using his experience to find quick but long-lasting solutions. He meets Allana, whose two Labradors Teddy and Fudge pull her along at break-neck speed when out walking. He then visits Peggy, a cross-breed with a penchant for escaping, no matter how much her owners try to keep her penned in. Finally, Graeme heads to Blackpool, where Stanley the terrier has been struggling ever since his best friend Sweep died

programme.title: Dogs Behaving (Very) Badly
programme.desc: Graeme Hall tries to help the owners of Bella the collie in Sheffield, whose aggression has become so extreme that people can no longer visit the house and the family cannot take the pet out in public. Graeme also meets Toby, a stroppy basset hound that enjoys sitting on the kitchen counter, and Dave, the French bulldog with a penchant for eating its own droppings

programme.title: Dogs Behaving (Very) Badly
programme.desc: Trainer Graeme Hall tries to solve more dog behavioural problems, visiting a family in East Sussex struggling with rescue puppy Diego, a Rottweiler-Rhodesian ridgeback cross. In Cambridgeshire, cocker spaniel Doug's relentless thievery is destroying any chance of a peaceful retirement for owners Julie and Mark, while Graeme's third stop is in Derby, where he meets two miniature schnauzers known locally as the Krays

programme.title: Bargain-Loving Brits in the Sun
programme.desc: The programme follows Graham, a pest controller who battles critters, bugs and vermin across the Costa del Sol, as he goes on a snake hunt and is called to an ant infestation. Plus, a pair of Welsh ex-pats who moved to Spain to run a bar, and the so-called 'cat lady of the Costa del Sol', who feeds more than 100 street cats as well as taking them to the vets to be neutered

programme.title: Bargain-Loving Brits in the Sun
programme.desc: West Midlander Vince rushes to get his new bar ready, having mistakenly thought he was paying for a working bar when it turns out he had actually just bought the lease on the building - leaving him to do the rest himself. Meanwhile, former dancer Bambi plans a racy show to raise cash for a local dog rescue shelter, and Andy tours the coast buying and selling old furniture

programme.title: Bargain-Loving Brits in the Sun
programme.desc: Cameras focus on Sheffield-born market trader Jacqui, who needs to make as much as she can at the tourist night markets on the Costa del Sol prom. There's also a look at cockney former publican Victor, who has spent the past 18 years living in his motor home. But with only his pension to live on, and his movable mansion in need of a refurb, will he have to sell up?

programme.title: Rich House, Poor House Changed My Life
programme.desc: Permanent make-up artist Katy and her husband Paul run a hugely successful beauty clinic in East Sussex. They and their three young children - Sebastian, Maddox and Annabel - swapped homes with struggling single mum Nadine, her young son Morgan and her best friend Alex. Over the course of the week, both groups went on a huge emotional journey, and life changed for ever for Nadine

programme.title: World's Most Expensive Weddings
programme.desc: Documentary following three wealthy couples getting married in Singapore. The first episode focuses on Rachel and Ken, who are planning a lavish underwater-themed wedding featuring a huge walk-through aquarium, a mermaid hen party, and the bride emerging from a giant clamshell, while still incorporating traditional elements including a Chinese tea ceremony

programme.title: Million Pound Homes
programme.desc: A look at the country's most impressive motorhomes, beginning with Charlotte and David's. They share a passion for travel and now are having their own overlander built, an off-road motorhome that can deal with any terrain. Plus, a look at The Icon, built by Formula One champion Jacques Villeneuve, which rents out for £4,000 a night

programme.title: Basic Instinct (1992)
programme.desc: A hard-drinking San Francisco cop investigates the case of a man murdered with an ice-pick, but his normally well-honed intuition starts letting him down as he falls for the sultry and manipulative chief suspect, the victim's former girlfriend. Thriller from director Paul Verhoeven, starring Sharon Stone, Michael Douglas, George Dzundza and Jeanne Tripplehorn

They initially use their powers for self-improvement, but eventually start to turn against their classmates, driving the ringleader insane as her lust for power takes over. Horror, starring Robin Tunney, Fairuza Balk, Neve Campbell, Rachel True and Skeet Ulrich

programme.title: Countdown to Murder
programme.desc: Documentary examining some of Britain's most notorious murders, charting the last few days leading up to the killing from the perspectives of both perpetrator and victim, using dramatic reconstruction to pinpoint the trigger that set each one in motion, providing in-depth psychological analysis and scrutiny of the devious and calculating plans the killers formulated. This episode examines the events leading up to the death of Natalie Jarvis in 2012, who was murdered by Adam Whelehan, a man with whom she had been having a casual relationship
